title: Almond Cake
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 3/4 pound lard
- 5 eggs
- 3/4 teaspoonful alkaline solution
- almond

## Directions

1. Mix the flour, sugar, lard, eggs, and solution well on a suitable board. Add a little quantity of lard at a time until every particle of flour will contain an equal amount of each substance.
2. Make into a cake of any desired size. In the center of each place an almond.
3. Put into a suitable pan and bake in the oven until nicely browned. The length of time depends on the temperature of the oven and the amount of cake.

title: Almond Chicken
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups almonds
- 2 cups onions cut into dice
- 9 cups water chestnuts cut into dice
- 2 cups mushrooms cut into dice
- 1 cup celery cut into dice
- 1 pound chicken, without bones

## Directions

1. Skin the almonds by putting in boiling water. Fry in boiling oil until they turn yellow.
2. Put the water chestnuts, onions, mushrooms, and celery in a hot cooking pan containing some oil, and fry rapidly. Keep them moving, so that each particle receives the same amount of heat. Add water enough to cover and boil for 15 minutes.
3. Put the chicken in a hot oiled pan and fry for 2 minutes. Add enough primary soup to cover, and cook, with the cover on, for 10 minutes.
4. Add Chinese gravy. Take from the stove at once.
5. Put the chicken on a dish and add the secondary vegetables (onions, water chestnuts, etc.). On top of this put the almonds.

title: Bean Biscuit
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound red beans
- 2 pounds flour
- ¾ cup lard
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 2 teaspoonfuls baking powder
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the beans in water for 3 hours, and mash. Place in water so that the skins of the beans float on top. Remove these and then filter the beans through a thick cloth and let dry. Now mix with the lard and a little sugar.
2. Sift the flour and mix with it enough cold water to make a thin dough.
3. Roll the dough out thin. Cut in the size of biscuits.
4. Roll these biscuits flat and fill each one with the beans, wrapping the dough around the beans to make a perfect ball. Close the dough up carefully.
5. Let the dough biscuits stand in a double-boiler for 10 minutes before putting on the fire. Steam for ¾ hour.

title: Bean Cake Chop Suey
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ pounds pork
- 10 pieces bean cake
- 2 cups onions
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the pork, bean cake, and the onions into small pieces.
2. Put them into an oiled pan and fry for 10 minutes.
3. Add enough water to cover. Cover the pan and cook for 15 minutes.
4. Add Chinese gravy.
5. Serve hot, in individual bowls.

title: Bean Cake Omelet Fo Yung Dow Fo
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- A little oil
- Salt
- 6 pieces bean cake
- Chinese ham dice
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with a little salt and oil.
2. Fry the bean cakes in an oiled pan. Add water and boil for 10 minutes.
3. Put plenty of oil in the pan, greasing it well all over. Put in the egg. Keep on turning until nearly done. Add the bean cake and cook until done.
4. Use Chinese ham dice and parsley for garnish.

title: Bean Sprouts
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- water

## Directions

1. Soak some beans in water in a flat dish.
2. Spread the beans out; do not let one cover another.
3. Cover with a piece of wet cloth, and water every morning.
4. The sprouts will appear in two nights.
5. They are ready to eat when they are 2 inches long.

title: Beef Chop Suey
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 pound beef
- 4 cups celery cut into pieces 3/4 inch long
- 2 cups bean sprouts
- 3/4 teaspoonful cornstarch
- 3/4 teaspoonful Fun Wine
- A few pieces sesamum-seed oil
- A few threads ginger root
- salt to taste
- Chinese sauce
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the beef into pieces 1/32 inch by 3/4 inch by 1 3/4 inches. Mix thoroughly with the cornstarch, sesamum-seed oil, Fun Wine, and a little salt and Chinese sauce.
2. Put the celery and bean sprouts into a hot, oiled pan and fry for 5 minutes.
3. Add water enough to cover. Spread the beef on top. Cover tightly and cook until nearly dry.
4. Add Chinese gravy and mix well.

title: Bird's Nest Soup
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of bird's nests
- one fat chicken or one lean chicken with two small fat pork chops
- two quarts of water
- onion
- white meat of the chicken

## Directions

1. Soak the bird's nests in water all night
2. Remove any trace of feathers and wash carefully in several waters
3. Boil a fat chicken in two quarts of water, or if a lean chicken, add two small fat pork chops
4. Cook till a good rich stock is secured and season lightly
5. Boil a small amount of onion with the chicken
6. Strain the stock
7. Boil the soaked bird's nests in this stock for twenty minutes
8. Cut a little of the white meat of the chicken in fine pieces
9. Serve in each bowl
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Bird-Nest in Chicken
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups bird-nest
- 1 large whole chicken (at least 7 pounds)
- 1 bowl primary soup
- 1 tablespoonful Chinese ham

## Directions

1. Soak the bird-nest in cold water for 1 hour. Then wash gently and cook for 1 hour with a piece of ginger.
2. Pick the chicken, chop off the head, feet and wings, cut 1 line 4 inches long and take out the interior. Clean the chicken and rub inside and outside with salt. Place in a bowl with the cut side on top.
3. Fill the chicken with the bird-nest and the primary soup. Steam for 3 hours in a double-boiler.
4. Now transfer into a second bowl by using a bowl a little larger than the first bowl and putting this second bowl on top of the first bowl mouth to mouth. Garnish with the Chinese ham dice.

title: Bird-Nest in Pigeon
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pigeons
- 2 cups bird-nest
- 1 cup primary soup
- 1 piece of ginger root

## Directions

1. Soak the bird-nest in cold water for 1 hour. Shake off any feathers, etc. Wash gently and boil for 1½ hours with the ginger.
2. Pick the pigeons. Chop off the head, feet and wings. Cut one line 3 inches long and take out the interior. Wash the pigeons and rub inside and out with salt. Place in a bowl with the cut side on top.
3. Fill the pigeons with the bird-nest and primary soup. Steam for 2½ hours in a double-boiler.
4. Now transfer into a larger bowl by putting the two bowls mouth to mouth. Garnish with Chinese ham dice.

title: Bird-Nest Soup
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups of bird-nest
- 1 piece of ginger
- 6 pints of primary soup
- 6 teaspoonfuls of Chinese ham and chicken dice
- 3 teaspoonfuls of cornstarch
- A few drops of sesamum-seed oil
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Soak in cold water for one hour 2 cups of bird-nest, then wash gently
2. Cook in water for 1 hour, with a piece of ginger
3. Strain off and put into 6 pints of primary soup
4. Let simmer for 1/2 hour
5. Serve in bowls or soup-plates, and use 6 teaspoonfuls of Chinese ham and chicken dice for garnish
6. For gravy use 3 teaspoonfuls of cornstarch, a few drops of sesamum-seed oil, and salt and pepper to taste

title: Boned Squab
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- small white mushrooms
- oil
- powdered spice
- Chinese Sauces

## Directions

1. Open the squab down the back with a very sharp knife
2. Remove the entire skeleton of the bird from the meat without breaking the skin
3. Carefully tie the ends of the wings, the legs and the neck
4. Sew up the back of the bird except one small opening
5. Coat the small white mushrooms with oil and rub a small portion of powdered spice over them
6. Insert the mushrooms through the small opening
7. Place the birds in a pan
8. Cook in a hot oven, frequently basting with a mixture of the Chinese Sauces
9. Allow twenty minutes for cooking
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Brittle Pork
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds pork cut into pieces 3/8 inch by 1 3/8 inches by 3/8 inch
- A few pieces of Octogon spicery
- 2 tablespoonfuls Fun Wine
- 1 egg
- 1 tablespoonful cornstarch

## Directions

1. Boil the pork and spicery in plain water for 10 minutes. Let cool.
2. Break the egg. Mix with the cornstarch. Then mix with the pork.
3. Fry in boiling oil until it gets red.
4. Put the fried pork into cold water and change water several times until no oil floats on the surface.
5. Dry the pork and place in a bowl.
6. Add the Fun Wine, and steam for 2 hours.

title: Checker-Board Eggs
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful Chinese ham
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 1 cup celery
- 1 cup water chestnuts
- oil
- salt
- primary soup
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the ham and secondary vegetables into threads 1½ inches long. Fry in an oiled pan. Mix well. Add enough primary soup to cover. Cook until done. Salt to suit the taste.
2. Beat the eggs well with oil and salt. Put them into a hot pan and let them run all over the pan evenly. Turn over.
3. Put this egg layer on a dish. Cover one-half of the layer with the fried ham, bamboo shoot, celery, and water chestnuts about ¾ inch thick. Then cover this with the other half of the egg layer.
4. Add Chinese gravy.

title: Chestnut Chicken
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup chicken
- 2 cups chestnuts
- 2 cups water chestnuts
- 2 cups mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut the chicken into pieces 3/8 inch by 1 3/8 inches. Cut the chestnuts, water chestnuts, and mushrooms into big pieces.
2. Rub salt and a little Chinese sauce on the chicken. Then put into a pot of boiling oil until the color becomes yellow.
3. Now put into primary soup and boil until nearly soft.
4. Add the secondary vegetables and cook until done.
5. Serve hot and add parsley for garnish.

title: Chicken Chop Sooy (With Giblets)
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound lean young pig pork, cut into slices one inch long, one-quarter inch wide and one-quarter inch thick
- Two chicken livers, gizzards and hearts, sliced thin
- One-half ounce green ginger roots
- One tablespoon olive oil
- One pound cooked white meat of chicken, cut fine
- One cupful hot chicken stock
- One tablespoon Seasoning Sauce
- Two cups celery, cut fine
- One small can white mushrooms, sliced
- One tablespoon shredded onion
- One-half cup bean sprouts

## Directions

1. Cut one pound lean young pig pork into slices one inch long, one-quarter inch wide and one-quarter inch thick.
2. Slice thin two chicken livers, gizzards and hearts, and one-half ounce green ginger roots.
3. Put all in kettle with one tablespoon olive oil previously heated, and brown evenly, stirring frequently till done, using care that it does not scorch.
4. Add one pound cooked white meat of chicken cut fine.
5. Pour around sides of kettle one cupful hot chicken stock, add one tablespoon Seasoning Sauce and stir carefully.
6. Have ready two cups celery cut fine, one small can white mushrooms sliced, a tablespoon shredded onion and put all in kettle, cooking for twelve minutes.
7. Put in one-half cup bean sprouts, adding a little as directed.
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Chicken Chop Suey
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken cut into fragments
- 3/4 cups water chestnuts
- 2 cups mushrooms
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 2 cups celery cut into thin pieces

## Directions

1. Put the water chestnuts, mushrooms, bamboo shoots, and celery into a hot pan containing oil. Fry for 2 minutes. Add water and cook for 15 minutes.
2. Put the chicken in a frying-pan containing oil and fry for 5 minutes.
3. Add the secondary vegetables and mix well.
4. Add enough primary soup to cover, and cook for 5 minutes.
5. Add Chinese gravy and stir. Remove from the stove at once.

title: Chicken Chop with Green Ginger
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- one teaspoonful each of olive oil and peanut oil
- one-half pound lean young pig pork cut in long thin strips
- one pound cooked chicken meat both dark and white
- one cupful bamboo shoots cut fine
- one cup white mushroom tops
- one-half ounce green ginger root
- one cup chicken stock
- one cup bean sprouts
- one teaspoon of each sauce
- one teaspoon rice flour
- six drops Sesamun oil

## Directions

1. Have a teasponful each of olive oil and peanut oil hot in the kettle
2. Put in first a half pound lean young pig pork cut in long thin strips and brown carefully
3. When done add one pound cooked chicken meat both dark and white
4. Add one cupful bamboo shoots cut fine, one cup white mushroom tops, one-half ounce green ginger root and pour around dish one cup chicken stock
5. Cook for ten minutes and add one cup bean sprouts
6. Cook six minutes longer, no more, or bean sprouts cook too long
7. Add one teaspoon of each sauce with one teaspoon rice flour for thickening
8. Add six drops Sesamun oil just before serving
9. Cut bamboo shoots and mushroom tops very fine
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Chicken Chop with Pineapple
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound young pig pork very fine cut
- peanut oil
- one small teaspoon shredded onion
- one pound cooked white meat of chicken cut very fine
- one-half cup Chinese potatoes
- one-half cup centers of celery stalks cut very fine
- one cupful weak chicken stock
- one teaspoon rice flour
- one dessertspoon Seasoning Sauce
- one-half cup imported Chinese Pineapple cut in thin strips

## Directions

1. Use one-half pound young pig pork, very fine cut, and browned in kettle with peanut oil
2. Add one small teaspoon shredded onion and one pound cooked white meat of chicken cut very fine
3. Add one-half cup Chinese potatoes, one-half cup centers of celery stalks, cut very fine, both
4. Pour around one cupful weak chicken stock and let all cook for twelve minutes
5. When done thicken with teaspoon rice flour and dessertspoon Seasoning Sauce
6. Just before removing from fire add one-half cup imported Chinese Pineapple cut in thin strips
7. Serve with small bowls of rice and tea
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Chicken Fried Noodles
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup onions cut into threads
- 2 cupfuls chicken cut into threads 1½ inches long
- 1 egg, fried and cut into threads
- 1 cup Chinese mushrooms
- 1 tablespoonful cornstarch
- 1 tablespoonful Chinese sauce
- 3 cups primary soup
- 2 tablespoonfuls lard
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Put the lard on the noodles, and steam for ¾ hour.
2. After the noodles have been steamed, they tend to stay together; therefore they should be loosened up with a fork.
3. Place the steamed noodles in a pan of boiling oil and fry until nicely brown. Then put on a dish.
4. Cook the onion and chicken until done.
5. Make a gravy of the primary soup, cornstarch (first dissolved in water), Chinese sauce, and salt and pepper. Add the mushrooms and the egg threads to the gravy.
6. Put the onion and chicken over the noodles. Pour over this the gravy.

title: Chicken Fruit Chop Sooy (Sum Soo)
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- One-half pound lean pork from the shoulder
- One-half pound white meat of cooked chicken
- Seven Chinese potatoes, peeled
- Six Chinese mushrooms, previously steamed with stems removed
- Scant cupful bamboo shoots
- One cupful celery
- One level tablespoon olive oil
- One cupful weak and hot chicken stock
- One teaspoon of each sauce
- One dessertspoon rice flour, wheat flour, or cornstarch
- One teaspoon chicken stock
- A pinch sugar

## Directions

1. Cut fine one-half pound of lean pork from the shoulder, one-half pound white meat of cooked chicken, seven Chinese potatoes, six Chinese mushrooms, scant cupful bamboo shoots, and one cupful celery.
2. Place pork in kettle with one level tablespoon olive oil already heated to prevent meat from sticking, and stir frequently till done.
3. Add the cooked chicken meat and the vegetables prepared as directed above, adding a little more oil if necessary.
4. Draw the mixture carefully from the sides of the kettle to the center and pour around the sides of the kettle a cupful of weak and hot chicken stock.
5. Cover tightly and let cook slowly till the vegetables are done for about fifteen minutes.
6. Mix one teaspoon of each sauce with one dessertspoon rice flour, wheat flour, or cornstarch, a teaspoon chicken stock, and a pinch sugar to thicken gravy.
7. Add a little more stock to the kettle if necessary.
8. Serve with bowl of rice and pot of tea.
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Chicken Hash
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken cut into very small pieces
- 2 cups each of water chestnuts, mushrooms, and bamboo shoots, chopped fine
- 3/8 teaspoonful of ginger juice
- 3/8 teaspoonful of Fun Wine
- 1 head of lettuce cut into threads
- 1 tablespoonful of Chinese ham cut into dice
- 1 handful of fried noodles

## Directions

1. Fry the chopped chicken in a hot, oiled pan.
2. Add the ginger juice and Fun Wine. Then the chopped water chestnuts, mushrooms, and bamboo shoots. Mix well.
3. Add enough primary soup to cover and cook until done.
4. Add Chinese gravy and remove from the stove at once.
5. Salt to suit the taste.
6. Serve on top of lettuce. Use the Chinese ham dice and the fried noodles as a garnish.

title: Chicken Mushroom Soup
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups Chinese mushrooms chopped into small dice
- 3/4 cup chicken chopped to same size
- 9 cups primary soup
- 2 eggs
- 3/4 teaspoonful cornstarch
- 1 teaspoonful Chinese sauce
- 3/4 teaspoonful oil
- Salt and a few drops of sesamum-seed oil

## Directions

1. Cook the mushrooms for 3/4 hour, and then drain off and put into the primary soup
2. Boil for 15 minutes
3. Add the chopped chicken, the eggs well beaten, and all the other ingredients
4. Take off the stove 1/2 minute after the cornstarch is added
5. Serve in bowls
6. Garnish the top of each bowl with 1 teaspoonful of Chinese ham cut into dice

title: Chicken Omelet Fo Yun Guy
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 1 1/2 pounds chicken
- 2 onions
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 3 cups water chestnuts
- 2 cups mushrooms

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs well and put them in a bowl with oil and salt.
2. Cut the onions, chicken, bamboo shoots, water chestnuts, and mushrooms into threads 1 1/2 inches long.
3. Mix together all of the ingredients and fry in boiling oil until done.
4. Put all in a bowl and add Chinese gravy.

title: Chicken Starch
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 breasts of chickens
- 1 cup primary soup
- 1 teaspoonful cornstarch
- white of one egg

## Directions

1. Pound the chicken, without skin and bone, as fine as possible. It is best when pounded with a hammer on a chopping-board.
2. Add the soup, cornstarch, and white of egg. Stir well.
3. Never pour it into the substance without first removing the pan from the fire. Keep stirring. Take off the fire the minute it begins to boil. The taste is bad if it boils too long.

title: Chicken Starch Bean Cake
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- 1 teaspoonful cornstarch
- white of an egg
- one cup primary soup
- five pieces bean cake
- boiling oil
- cold water
- Chinese ham dice
- milk
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pound chicken, without the skin and bone, as fine as possible using a hammer on a chopping-board.
2. Add to this 1 teaspoonful of cornstarch, white of an egg, and one cup of primary soup. Stir well.
3. Pour chicken starch into the substance before placing the pan on the stove. Keep stirring. Take the pan away from the fire just as it begins to boil.
4. Fry five pieces of bean cake in boiling oil for 5 minutes. Cut them into 1/4 inch cubes. Put in cold water until no oil floats on the top.
5. Mix well the bean cake with one-half as much Chinese ham dice and an equal amount of milk. Cook in a pan until just done.
6. Add the chicken starch, and sugar to suit the taste. Watch the heat carefully so that the chicken starch will not be too well done.

title: Chicken Thread
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken cut into threads
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 2 cups Chinese mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cook the bamboo shoots and mushrooms for 20 minutes. Then mix with the chicken.
2. Add enough primary soup to cover and cook for 5 minutes.
3. Add Chinese gravy and remove from the stove at once.

title: Chinese Chop Sooy (Ordinary)
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound lean pork
- A dessertspoonful of lard, olive oil, butter or peanut oil
- One small cupful sliced onions
- One cupful hot chicken stock or water
- Six Chinese mushrooms, soaked or steamed with stems removed
- Six Chinese potatoes, peeled and sliced thin
- Two cupfuls celery, sliced crosswise
- One tablespoon Seasoning Sauce
- Two tablespoons Flavoring Sauce
- A heaping teaspoon rice flour or cornstarch
- Four drops Sesamun Oil

## Directions

1. Cut one pound of lean pork in small pieces and braise in the kettle with a dessertspoonful of lard, olive oil, butter or peanut oil, the latter being preferred.
2. Cover tightly, removing the cover and stirring occasionally till cooked through and lightly browned.
3. Watch carefully to prevent burning.
4. Add one small cupful sliced onions and continue to cook for three or four minutes.
5. Pour a cupful of hot chicken stock or water, carefully around the sides of the kettle, taking great care not to pour over the top of the food.
6. Cut the Chinese mushrooms fine and the Chinese potatoes into thin slices.
7. Add the mushrooms, potatoes, and celery immediately to the pork and onions.
8. Put in one tablespoon of the Seasoning Sauce and mix well with the ingredients and cover the kettle tightly.
9. Let cook for twelve or fifteen minutes slowly, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking to bottom of kettle and burning.
10. Add water or stock, as necessary, to prevent the mixture from becoming dry.
11. Mix two tablespoons of the Flavoring Sauce with a heaping teaspoon of rice flour or cornstarch and add to the ingredients in the kettle, allowing the gravy to thicken slowly.
12. Add four drops of Sesamun Oil, stir carefully and remove from the fire.
13. Serve with rice in separate bowls and with tea.
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Chinese Cured Pork
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- pork, preferably from the shoulder of the pig
- Seasoning Sauce
- Flavoring Sauce
- good brandy

## Directions

1. Remove the fat from the pork, cut preferably from the shoulder of the pig, and cut into strips.
2. Rub into the pork the Seasoning Sauce, and a little of the Flavoring Sauce.
3. Rub over it some good brandy and place in the oven.
4. Mix a small cup of the two sauces, add a little brandy, and baste frequently till done.
5. This cured pork is used for a garnish for many of the dishes, and is sliced in thin strips about two inches long.
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Chinese Cured Pork Cakes
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cupful Chinese cured pork
- one-fourth cupful bamboo shoots
- a half cupful celery
- a half cupful Chinese potatoes
- a little shredded onion
- a half dozen eggs
- One teaspoonful of each of the two sauces
- a small half teaspoonful of sugar
- a teaspoonful of rice flour or cornstarch
- a little water

## Directions

1. Cut into thin shreds one-half cupful Chinese cured pork, one-fourth cupful bamboo shoots, a half cupful each of celery and Chinese potatoes, and a little shredded onion.
2. After all is cut, put in bowl and break over it a half dozen eggs.
3. Mix all together and fry in small cakes that are flat like pancakes.
4. Serve on an oval platter and pour over it the following sauce.
5. Mix one teaspoonful of each of the two sauces, a small half teaspoonful of sugar, a teaspoonful of rice flour or cornstarch and a little water in pan in which cakes were fried.
6. Pour sauce over cakes in platter.
7. Serve with rice and tea.
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Chinese Frankfurter
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- Outside lining of the small intestine of a pig
- Pork
- Salt
- Sweet sauce
- Fun Wine
- Orange skin

## Directions

1. Get the outside lining of the small intestine of a pig. Wash thoroughly with salt and then put into hot water for 10 minutes. Tie one end, force air through from the other end, and tie. Dry in the hot sun.
2. Cut pork into pieces ⅜ inch by ⅜ inch by ⅜ inch. To every pound of pork add 3 ounces of salt, 1 tablespoonful of sweet sauce, 1 tablespoonful of Fun Wine, and a few threads of orange skin. Mix well.
3. By means of a funnel, put this mixture into the pig lining. Tie the skin about every 6 inches. Punch plenty of small holes with a needle.
4. Expose in the hot sun for 1 day. Put in a windy place for 4 days. The air should be dry, so it is best to make these in the fall or winter.
5. Keep in a china jar. At least they must be kept in a jar for 5 days before being eaten.
6. Steam them in a double-boiler for ¾ hour before serving. Serve with fried potatoes or fried gray potatoes underneath.

title: Chinese Frankfurters On Rice
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups rice
- 6 pairs Chinese frankfurters

## Directions

1. Wash the rice 3 times. Put in a covered pan with 7½ cups water and cook until boiled.
2. Place the Chinese frankfurters on top of the rice. When dry keep over a low fire. Move the pan frequently to prevent burning. Take from the stove and keep the cover on for 10 minutes.
3. Take out the Chinese frankfurters and cut into the required size.
4. Add salt, mix well.

title: Chinese Frankfurters With Vegetables
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 pairs Chinese frankfurters
- Any Chinese vegetables (twice the amount of the frankfurters)

## Directions

1. Cut the vegetables into pieces 1 inch by 1 inch by 1½ inches. Fry in an oiled pan with salt until the volume is considerably reduced. Add water enough to cover.
2. Add the frankfurters. Cover and cook until nearly dry.
3. Add Chinese gravy.

title: Chinese Fritters
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of rice flour
- one-half cup sugar
- hot water

## Directions

1. Scald the rice flour and sugar together with hot water
2. Mix like bread, kneading lightly
3. Roll into little balls
4. Cook in deep fat as you would ordinary fritters or doughnuts
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Chinese Ginger Salad
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- six Mandarin oranges
- two small cucumbers
- two good sized preserved ginger roots
- a small piece of preserved citron
- one-half cupful of the syrup of the preserved ginger root
- an equal amount of lemon juice
- salted almonds

## Directions

1. Slice six Mandarin oranges, removing all skin and seeds
2. Peel and cut into cubes two small cucumbers
3. Mince fine two good sized preserved ginger roots
4. Boil a small piece of preserved citron till tender
5. Mix together all the prepared ingredients
6. Mix a half cupful of the syrup of the preserved ginger root with an equal amount of lemon juice
7. Pour the syrup mixture over the salad
8. Garnish with salted almonds and serve
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Chinese Gravy
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup primary soup
- 1 teaspoonful cornstarch
- 3/4 teaspoonful Chinese sauce
- salt
- sugar
- a few drops of sesamum-seed oil

## Directions

1. Mix the cornstarch well in a little cold water.
2. Stir into the boiling primary soup, and let boil until it thickens.
3. Add the Chinese sauce, salt, sugar, and sesamum-seed oil, and stir well.

title: Chinese Meat Biscuit
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 cups flour
- 1 yeast cake
- 2 small tablespoonfuls salt
- 1 tablespoonful sugar
- 3 potatoes
- 1 tablespoonful lard

## Directions

1. Wash and pare the potatoes. Cover with cold water. Boil until tender. Mash the potatoes in the same water in which they are boiled. Let stand until just luke-warm.
2. Put in the salt and sugar.
3. Add the lard and yeast cake. Dissolve thoroughly.
4. Sift the flour thoroughly and put into a pan. Make a hole in the middle of it. Slowly pour in the potato mixture. Mix well.
5. Dredge the hands well with flour. Turn the dough out on the mixing-board. Use as little flour as possible on the hands when kneading the dough — only enough to keep the dough from sticking to the hands. Knead for 10 minutes.
6. Put into a pan. Cover well.

title: Chinese Methods of Cooking Rice
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- water

## Directions

1. Wash the rice in many waters, rubbing the rice well between the hands in each water until it is entirely free from the white powdery substance that gives the water a milky appearance
2. Continue washing until the water remains perfectly clear
3. Throw the washed rice into a good sized kettle in which a considerable quantity of water is boiling rapidly
4. Allow this rapid boiling to continue for at least a quarter of an hour, or more
5. Take one kernel of rice from the kettle and rub between the thumb and finger to test doneness; if it rubs away easily, the rice is done; if not quite done boil a few minutes longer
6. Pour the contents of the kettle into a colander with fine holes set in a shallow pan and let the water drain off
7. Put the rice back into the kettle, pour over it a small cup of boiling water
8. Cover the kettle tightly and set near, but not over the fire till time to serve
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Chinese Red Cheese
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- Bean cake made of Chinese red beans
- Fun Wine
- Salt

## Directions

1. Wrap up the cakes in a piece of cloth in any desired size
2. Put pressure on top for 5 days
3. Take off the weight and then the cloth
4. Scrape off the mold on top
5. Place in a jar
6. Fill the jar 1/2 full of Fun Wine, and add plenty of salt
7. Cover air-tight, and set away for not less than two weeks; the longer, the better, provided the jar is kept air-tight

title: Chinese Roast Pig
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- Young sucking pig
- Seasoning Sauce
- finely ground imported spice

## Directions

1. The pig is carefully dressed, but is not stuffed.
2. Rub inside and out with the Seasoning Sauce until the meat is well seasoned.
3. Rub with the finely ground imported spice.
4. Hang on a spit over a clear wood fire.
5. Turn frequently and cook slowly till brown and crisp.
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Chinese Sauce
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- Chinese white beans
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil Chinese white beans slowly for 6 hours.
2. Strain off the beans and expose the bean soup in a big pot placed under the hot sun.
3. The surface of the liquid turns brown and has a top layer. Remove this layer of brown.
4. A little later take off the other layers, and so on until there is no brown layer.
5. Add salt to this, and boil.

title: Chinese Sponge Cake
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 eggs
- 1 pound sugar
- 5/8 pound flour
- A few drops of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs in a suitable bowl. Mix well with sugar. Beat for an hour, being careful always to beat in one direction.
2. Mix with the flour and lemon juice.
3. Put into a suitable pan and steam for 3/4 hour.

title: Chinese Steamed Rice
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- water

## Directions

1. Wash the rice according to the previous directions
2. Put the rice in a kettle and pour over it sufficient boiling water to cover it at least two inches above the rice
3. Cover the kettle tightly so no steam can escape and set where it will keep very hot but not actually boil
4. Allow from half to three-quarters of an hour for this method of cooking
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Chinese White Cheese
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- Bean cake made of Chinese white beans
- Fun Wine
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut bean cake into half-inch squares 1/4 inch thick
2. Put into a jar provided with an air-tight cover
3. Fill the jar 1/4 full of Fun Wine
4. Salt to taste
5. Cover air-tight, and put away for not less than two weeks

title: Chop Sooy with Beef Tenderloin
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound tenderloin of beef cut in small square pieces
- one tablespoon olive oil
- one cupful sliced onions
- one cupful hot water
- one teaspoon peanut oil
- one cupful Chinese potatoes
- one cupful celery
- one cupful mushrooms
- one tablespoon Seasoning Sauce
- one dessertspoon corn starch or rice flour
- one tablespoon Flavoring Sauce

## Directions

1. Cut one pound tenderloin of beef in small square pieces and brown slowly and carefully in a tablespoon of olive oil already heated in kettle
2. When done add one cupful sliced onions and allow to brown slightly with beef for five minutes more
3. Pour carefully around sides of dish a cupful of hot water and a teaspoon of peanut oil
4. Have prepared one cupful Chinese potatoes, and the same of celery and mushrooms and add to the kettle
5. Cover tightly and cook for at least fifteen minutes
6. For seasoning, use one tablespoon Seasoning Sauce about five minutes before done
7. Mix a dessertspoon corn starch or rice flour in a tablespoon Flavoring Sauce and thicken gravy
8. Remove from the fire and serve with generous bowls of rice and unlimited tea
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Chop Sooy with Duck
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound partly fat young pork
- one tablespoon olive or peanut oil
- one pound cooked breast of duck cut in long fine strips
- one tablespoon shredded onion
- one cup fine cut Chinese potatoes
- one cup celery cut fine
- one cup tops of white mushrooms
- one cup hot chicken stock

## Directions

1. Have ready one-half pound partly fat young pork, but not too fat, and slice in thin strips
2. Brown in kettle with tablespoon olive or peanut oil
3. Add one pound cooked breast of duck cut in long fine strips, one tablespoon shredded onion and let cook five minutes
4. Add one cup fine cut Chinese potatoes, one cup celery cut fine, one cup tops of white mushrooms
5. Pour around sides one cup hot chicken stock and let cook fifteen minutes
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Chop Sooy with Green Peppers
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound lean pork cut fine
- one tablespoon olive oil
- one cup of the tops of French mushrooms cut very fine
- two green peppers cut fine with seeds removed
- one cup celery
- one-half cup bamboo shoots cut fine
- one tablespoon minced Spanish onion
- one teaspoon peanut oil
- one cup hot water
- one tablespoon Seasoning Sauce
- one rounded teaspoon corn starch or flour
- one teaspoon Flavoring Sauce
- four drops Sesamun oil

## Directions

1. Brown one pound lean pork cut fine in Chop Sooy kettle with one tablespoon olive oil
2. When done add one cup of the tops of French mushrooms cut very fine, two green peppers cut fine with seeds removed, one cup celery and one-half cup bamboo shoots cut fine and one tablespoon minced Spanish onion
3. When the vegetables are mixed with the meat, add one teaspoon peanut oil and one cup hot water, remembering to pour it carefully around sides of the kettle
4. Cover tightly and let all cook for fifteen minutes, very slow
5. Use one tablespoon Seasoning Sauce thickened with one rounded teaspoon corn starch or flour and add when vegetables are done
6. Then add one teaspoon Flavoring Sauce and four drops Sesamun oil
7. Serve with bowls of rice and tea
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Chop Sooy with Lamb
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound lean leg of lamb cut in cubes or small dice
- one dessertspoon peanut oil
- one small cup fine cut French mushrooms
- one cup celery cut fine
- one cup Spanish onions sliced thin
- one cup hot water
- one tablespoon Seasoning Sauce
- one teaspoon rice flour or cornstarch
- one teaspoon Flavoring Sauce
- a few drops Sesamun oil

## Directions

1. Cut one pound lean leg of lamb in cubes or small dice
2. Brown and cook carefully on one dessertspoon peanut oil
3. When done mix with lamb in kettle one small cup fine cut French mushrooms, one cup celery cut fine, one cup Spanish onions sliced thin
4. Add carefully one cup hot water, cover and let cook fifteen minutes
5. Use one tablespoon Seasoning Sauce with one teaspoon rice flour or cornstarch for thickening
6. When done, add one teaspoon Flavoring Sauce and a few drops Sesamun oil
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Chop Sooy with Veal
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound pork with little fat
- one tablespoon olive oil
- one pound lean veal cut in small dice
- one cup Chinese potatoes cut fine
- one-half cup bamboo shoots
- one-half cup Chinese mushrooms cut in fine pieces
- one cup weak chicken stock
- one tablespoon each kind of sauce
- one cupful bean sprouts
- one dessertspoon wheat or rice flour

## Directions

1. Cut one-half pound pork with little fat, not too much, fine and brown in kettle with one tablespoon olive oil
2. Brown for five minutes, then add one pound lean veal cut in small dice and brown all together till veal is done
3. Cover kettle tightly, taking off cover two or three times to stir meat
4. When done, have ready one cup Chinese potatoes cut fine, one-half cup bamboo shoots, one-half cup Chinese mushrooms all cut in fine pieces
5. Pour around one cup weak chicken stock, add the vegetables and cook for twelve minutes
6. Put in one tablespoon each kind of sauce, and one cupful bean sprouts
7. Cook for six minutes longer, thicken with one dessertspoon wheat or rice flour and serve with rice
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Chow Mein
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- noodles
- olive oil

## Directions

1. Do not allow the noodles to become too dry after making
2. If too much time is needed, place noodles in ice box till ready
3. Put olive oil in pan having the oil about one inch deep when heated
4. Spread the noodles over bottom of pan and let fry slowly till a golden color
5. Turn over with wide bladed knife or pancake turner and let fry on the other side till done, perhaps ten to fifteen minutes
6. Remove noodles and drain carefully to remove oil
7. Put on platter and serve with any one of the Chop Sooy dishes preferred
8. The Chop Sooy must be ready and hot, and the noodles served immediately when done
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Crab In Golden Pond
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 eggs
- 3 pounds crab
- 1 tablespoonful Chinese ham dice
- cold boiled water
- salt
- oil
- parsley
- sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the shell from the crab. Cut the meat into small pieces.
2. Mix the eggs with an equal amount of cold, boiled water. Beat well. Salt to suit the taste.
3. Put the eggs in a suitable bowl. Spread the crab on top. Steam for 20 minutes.
4. Garnish with the ham and parsley, and serve with oil and sauce.

title: Crab Omelet Fo Yung High
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 crabs
- 12 eggs
- 3 onions
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 2 cups water chestnuts
- 2 cups mushrooms

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs well and put them in a bowl with oil and salt.
2. Cut the onions, crabs, bamboo shoots, water chestnuts, and mushrooms into threads 1 1/2 inches long.
3. Mix together all of the ingredients and fry in boiling oil until done.
4. Put all in a bowl and add Chinese gravy.

title: Crab Soup
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 crabs
- 1 cup water chestnuts
- 1 cup bamboo shoots
- 1 cup mushrooms
- 7 pints primary soup

## Directions

1. Remove the shells from the crabs and put into boiling water for ½ hour. By means of a fork take out the meat and break into small pieces.
2. Cut secondary vegetables into pieces. Cook for 20 minutes.
3. Now put the secondary vegetables in with the crab.
4. Add primary soup and cook for 15 minutes.
5. Add Chinese gravy, mix well, and remove from the stove at once. Stir in parsley and garnish.

title: Deer
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds deer
- 2 cups water chestnuts
- 2 cups mushrooms
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 1 pound chicken meat
- 3/8 teaspoonful ginger juice
- 1 tablespoonful Fun Wine
- A few pieces of sugar-cane about 3 inches long, unskinned
- salt
- primary soup
- oil
- Chinese sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the deer into pieces 1 1/2 inches by 1 1/2 inches by 1 inch. Cut the secondary vegetables into small pieces.
2. Fry the deer in a hot, oiled pan. Turn constantly.
3. Add the ginger juice, sugar-cane, and a little salt. Add the secondary vegetables and the chicken and cook for 5 minutes.
4. Add enough primary soup to cover and bring to a boil.
5. Transfer to a suitable bowl. Put the Fun Wine on top. Steam for 2 hours.
6. Serve with oil and Chinese sauce.

title: Dried Pork With Fried Bean Cake And Chinese Vegetables
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- pork
- bean cakes
- Chinese vegetables

## Directions

1. Cut 1 pound pork into pieces 1 inch by 1 inch by 1 1/2 inches.
2. Use about 10 bean cakes. Cut each bean cake into 4 pieces. Fry until yellow.
3. Cut about 3 pounds of any Chinese vegetables into pieces the same size as the pork.
4. Cook the pork, bean cakes, and vegetables in plain water in an oiled pan for 3/4 hour.
5. Add Chinese gravy.

title: Dry Duck On Rice
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups rice
- 7 cups water
- dry duck
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash thoroughly 3 cups rice. Put it in a covered pan, add 7 cups of water, and boil until the rice is done.
2. Put dry duck on top of the rice and cook until the water has evaporated. Shake the pan frequently to prevent burning. Remove from the stove and keep the cover on for 10 minutes.
3. Take out the duck and cut into the required size.
4. Add salt. Mix well.

title: Dry Flat Fish Chop Suey
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- dry flat fish
- 1/2 teaspoonful ginger juice
- 1 tablespoonful Fun Wine
- 1 teaspoonful crab juice
- 2 cups dry mushrooms
- 1 cup celery
- 1 pound any vegetable
- a few pieces of pork
- 1 Chinese onion
- primary soup
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Soak the dry flat fish for 3/4 hour. Wash thoroughly. Take off a piece of bone on the center of one side of it. On the bone side put knife-marks one-half as deep as it is thick. Then cut into 2 inches by 1 1/4 inches.
2. Put into the cut flat fish 1/2 teaspoonful of ginger juice, 1 tablespoonful of Fun Wine, 1 teaspoonful of crab juice.
3. Soak 2 cups of dry mushrooms for 3/4 hour.
4. Cut 1 cup of celery, 1 pound of any vegetable, a few pieces of pork, and a Chinese onion into pieces 1 1/2 inches long.
5. Cook the mushrooms, celery, vegetables, and pork in an oiled pan with primary soup until done.
6. Put the fish into a very hot, oiled pan. Keep turning until it is rolled up like a piece of cigarette. Add the other ingredients and mix well.
7. Add Chinese gravy.

title: Dry Flat Fish Soup
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound dry flat fish
- 3/4 teaspoonful alkaline solution
- 12 cups primary soup
- sesamum-seed oil

## Directions

1. Cover the dry flat fish with cold water. Add the alkaline solution. Soak for 15 minutes. Wash thoroughly, take out the bones.
2. Cook the fish in plain water for 3 hours. Change the water several times while cooking. Then cook for 15 minutes in the primary soup.
3. Serve with the soup. Put into it a few drops of sesamum-seed oil.

title: Dry Pork On Rice
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- rice
- dry pork
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash the rice 3 times. Put in a covered pan with 7 cups of water and cook until done.
2. Place the pork on top of the rice. When dry remove to the back of the stove. Shake the pan frequently to prevent burning. Remove from the stove and keep the cover on for 10 minutes.
3. Take out the pork and cut into the required size.
4. Add salt and mix well.

title: Dry Pork With Gray Potatoes
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- pork
- spicery salt
- salt
- sugar
- sauce residue
- sweet sauce
- Fun Wine
- Chinese tissue-paper
- bean cakes
- gray potatoes

## Directions

1. To each pound of pork use 2 ounces of spicery salt, 1 1/2 teaspoonfuls of salt, 1 1/2 teaspoonfuls of sugar. Mix and rub over the pork. Expose to the hot sun for 1 day. Dip into 5 ounces of sauce residue. Mash. Add 1 1/2 ounces of sweet sauce, 2 ounces of good Fun Wine. Mix well.
2. Wrap up with Chinese tissue-paper, and put in a dry place where it can stay until dry. It is now ready for cooking.
3. Cut 1 pound of dry pork into pieces 1 inch by 1 inch by 1 1/2 inches.
4. Use about 2 pounds of bean cakes. Cut each cake into 4 pieces. Fry until yellow.
5. Cut about 3 pounds of gray potatoes into pieces the same size as the pork.
6. Cook the pork, bean cakes, and gray potatoes in plain water in an oiled pan for 3/4 hour.

title: Duck
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half pound partly fat young pork
- tablespoon olive or peanut oil
- one pound cooked breast of duck
- one tablespoon shredded onion
- one cup fine cut Chinese potatoes
- one cup celery
- one cup tops of white mushrooms
- one cup hot chicken stock
- one tablespoon Seasoning and Flavoring Sauce
- one tablespoon Flavoring Sauce
- one dessertspoon corn starch or flour
- one-half ounce green ginger
- two green peppers

## Directions

1. Slice the pork in thin strips
2. Brown the pork in a kettle with tablespoon olive or peanut oil
3. Add one pound cooked breast of duck cut in long, fine strips, one tablespoon shredded onion and let cook five minutes
4. Add one cup fine cut Chinese potatoes, one cup celery cut fine, one cup tops of white mushrooms
5. Pour around sides one cup hot chicken stock and let cook fifteen minutes
6. Season with one tablespoon each of Seasoning and Flavoring Sauce and a dessertspoon corn starch, or flour for thickening
7. Add one-half ounce green ginger if liked, or two green peppers seeded and cut very fine
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Duck Hash
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck chopped into pieces as small as possible
- 2 cups water chestnuts chopped fine
- 2 cups mushrooms chopped fine
- 3/8 teaspoonful ginger juice
- 3/8 teaspoonful Fun Wine
- 1 head of lettuce cut into threads
- 2 cups fried noodles
- primary soup
- Chinese gravy
- salt
- Chinese ham dice

## Directions

1. Fry the chopped duck in a hot, oiled pan. Add the ginger juice and Fun Wine.
2. Then add the chopped water chestnuts, mushrooms, and bamboo shoots. Mix well. Add enough primary soup to cover and cook until done.
3. Add Chinese gravy, and salt to suit the taste. Serve on top of lettuce. Use the Chinese ham dice and the fried noodles as a garnish.

title: Duck Soup
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 medium-sized duck without bones
- 1 1/2 cups Chinese mushrooms
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 4 cups celery
- 2 teaspoonfuls oil
- A few drops sesamum-seed oil
- 1 teaspoonful Chinese sauce
- 1/4 teaspoonful cornstarch
- Salt
- Primary soup
- Chinese ham dice

## Directions

1. Chop all into dice
2. Heat the cooking pan for 3/4 minute
3. Then grease it thoroughly with oil
4. Add salt and all other ingredients except duck
5. Keep turning for about 5 minutes
6. Add primary soup and boil very slowly
7. Mix together well the duck (cut into dice), the oil, sesamum-seed oil, Chinese sauce, and cornstarch
8. When primary soup has boiled 3/4 hour, add the duck mixture
9. Boil slowly for another 3/4 hour
10. Use Chinese ham dice as a garnish

title: Duck Threads
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 2 cups mushrooms
- 3 cups primary soup
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Cook the duck until tender and tear off the meat into shreds.
2. Cook the bamboo shoots and mushrooms for 20 minutes. Then mix with the duck.
3. Add the primary soup and cook a while longer.
4. Add Chinese gravy, mix well, and serve.

title: East Melon Duck
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 boneless duck cut into small dice
- 1 east melon (8 inches in diameter)
- 1 handful skinned water chestnuts cut into small dice
- 1 handful lotus nuts
- 1 handful mushrooms cut into small dice
- 1 small piece of ginger without the skin, pounded
- 1 teaspoonful Fun Wine
- primary soup
- salt
- oil
- cold water

## Directions

1. Cut off the top of the melon as shown in the picture. Take out the seeds.
2. Oil the cooking-pan and heat.
3. Fry the duck for 10 minutes.
4. Add the ginger and Fun Wine. Salt. Fry for 1 minute more. Turn frequently.
5. Add chestnuts, lotus nuts, mushrooms, and enough primary soup to fill the melon. Cook until the soup boils.
6. Transfer all into the melon. Cover with the piece you cut from the melon. Steam for about 3½ hours; the size of melon decides the length of time necessary for steaming.
7. Remove from the stove. Add cold water to stop the steam. Take the melon out of the pan. Take off the cover of the melon. By means of a spoon take off any oil which has formed on the top.
8. Scoop out the melon and mix well with the duck and other ingredients. Be careful not to break the rind. Serve hot.

title: Eel In Net
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds eel
- 1½ cups skinless chestnuts
- 1 teaspoonful Fun Wine
- White of 1 egg
- Lard (leaf lard) enough to wrap the eel

## Directions

1. Put the eel into warm water for 20 minutes. Take out and wash. Cut into pieces 1 inch long. Place in boiling oil and fry until yellow.
2. Wrap each fried piece of eel with the lard. (This lard is the fat of a pig and looks like net.) Use the white of egg for paste.
3. Put the wrapped eel into a pan. Add two cups more than enough water to cover. Add the chestnuts and Fun Wine. Cook until nearly dry (with about 2 cups of liquid remaining).
4. Add Chinese gravy. Garnish with parsley.

title: Egg Roll Dan Gun
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls dry mushrooms
- 2 tablespoonfuls bean sprouts
- 2 tablespoonfuls chicken meat
- 2 tablespoonfuls Chinese ham

## Directions

1. Break the eggs into a bowl. Beat well with oil and salt. Have a low fire. Oil pan. Place in it one tablespoonful of well-beaten egg. Take hold of the pan and let the egg run all over the surface of the pan. A layer of egg will be formed.
2. Cut the mushrooms, Chinese ham, bean sprouts, and chicken into threads. Fry for 5 minutes in an oiled pan. Salt to suit the taste. Add enough water to cover and cook until dry. When nearly dry, turn constantly to prevent burning.
3. Roll this in the egg, making the roll 1 inch in diameter and as long as the egg layer. Use white of egg for paste. Cut carefully into the desired length.
4. Add Chinese gravy.

title: Extra Chop Suey
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds pork cut into pieces 1/16 inch by 3/8 inch by 1 inch
- Bean sprouts equal to amount of pork
- 2 cups onions cut into threads
- 2 cups bamboo shoots cut into pieces same size as pork
- 2 cups mushrooms
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Put the pork, bean sprouts, onions, bamboo shoots and mushrooms into an oiled pan and fry for 10 minutes.
2. Add water enough to cover and cook for 15 minutes.
3. Add Chinese gravy.
4. Remove from the stove and serve at once.

title: Fish Balls
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds pike
- 1 1/2 cups salted almonds or peanuts
- 3/4 cup Chinese ham
- 6 pounds vegetables in season
- 1 teaspoonful cornstarch
- 1 cup water
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the pike and take out the bones. Run through the grinder three times.
2. Cut into small pieces the almonds, ham, and the vegetables.
3. Put the pike into a big bowl and mix with it the cornstarch, 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoonful salt. Stir well for an hour. Be sure to stir in the same direction all the time.
4. Add the nuts and ham. Mix well.
5. Wash your hands as clean as possible. Hold the mixture in your hand and squeeze gently; a small ball will come through the top of the fingers. Take off with a spoon. Wash the spoon each time with cold water.
6. Place the ball in a pan of boiling water. When it comes to the top it indicates it is done and can be placed in a bowl.
7. Cook the vegetables until done.
8. Mix together the fish balls, the vegetables and Chinese gravy.

title: Fish Chop Suey
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 pounds pike not less than 2 1/2 inches in diameter
- 2 cups mushrooms
- A few pieces of ginger cut into pieces 1/32 inch by 3/4 inch by 1 inch
- 3 cups bamboo shoots cut into pieces 3/4 by 1 3/4 inches by 1/32 inch
- 2 cups bean sprouts
- Chinese gravy
- parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the pike and take out the bones. Cut into pieces 1/16 inch thick.
2. Fry the mushrooms, ginger, bamboo shoots, and bean sprouts in a hot, oiled pan for 5 minutes.
3. Add water enough to cover all. Cook for 15 minutes.
4. Add Chinese gravy.
5. Add the pike and mix well. Garnish with parsley, and serve hot.

title: Fish Roll
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 4 pounds pike
- 1 tablespoonful Chinese ham
- 1 cup peanuts, pounded
- 1 cup Chinese roast pork
- oil
- salt
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Break the eggs into a bowl. Beat well with oil and salt. Have a low fire. Oil pan. Place into it 1 tablespoonful well beaten egg. Take hold of the pan and let the egg run over the surface of the pan. A layer of egg will be formed.
2. Remove the skin from the pike and take out the bones. Grind the meat through a fine grinder several times, or pound with a hammer as fine as possible. Beat well for ¾ hour with salt and a small amount of oil.
3. Cut the Chinese ham and pork into dice. Mix well with the remaining egg, the pike, and the peanuts.
4. Roll this mixture into the egg layer, having the layer 1 inch in diameter. Steam for ¾ hour.
5. Cut into the desired length and add Chinese gravy.

title: Fish Soup
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 five-pound fish
- 5 pints primary soup
- 2 cups water chestnuts
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 1 cup Chinese mushrooms

## Directions

1. Dress the fish and cook until soft. Take out bones. Tear the meat to pieces (1½ inches long).
2. Cut the secondary vegetables into dice. Cook for 20 minutes.
3. Put the vegetables and the fish into the primary soup, and cook for 15 minutes.
4. Add Chinese gravy. Stir the soup well and take from the stove. Serve in bowls. Garnish each bowl.

title: Fish Swimming In A Golden Pond
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 eggs
- 5 pounds pike
- 1 tablespoonful Chinese ham dice
- cold boiled water
- salt
- oil
- parsley
- sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the pike. Cut the meat into small pieces.
2. Mix the eggs with an equal amount of cold, boiled water. Beat well. Salt to suit the taste.
3. Put the eggs in a suitable bowl. Spread the pike on top. Steam for 20 minutes.
4. Garnish with the ham and parsley, and serve with oil and sauce.

title: Foo Tong
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cupful Chinese cured pork
- one-fourth cupful bamboo shoots
- a half cupful each of celery and Chinese potatoes
- a little shredded onion
- a half dozen eggs
- one teaspoonful of each of the two sauces
- a small half teaspoonful of sugar
- a teaspoonful of rice flour or cornstarch
- a little water
- rice

## Directions

1. Cut into thin shreds one-half cupful Chinese cured pork, one-fourth cupful bamboo shoots, a half cupful each of celery and Chinese potatoes, and a little shredded onion
2. Put in bowl and break over it a half dozen eggs
3. Mix all together
4. Fry in small cakes that are flat like pancakes
5. Mix one teaspoonful of each of the two sauces, a small half teaspoonful of sugar, a teaspoonful of rice flour or cornstarch and a little water in the pan in which cakes were fried
6. Serve cakes on an oval platter and pour sauce over it
7. Serve with rice
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Foo Yong with Chicken
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cupful cooked meat of chicken
- one-fourth cupful bamboo shoots
- a half cupful celery
- a half cupful Chinese potatoes
- a little shredded onion
- a half dozen eggs
- One teaspoonful of each of the two sauces
- a small half teaspoonful of sugar
- a teaspoonful of rice flour or cornstarch
- a little water

## Directions

1. Cut into fine pieces one-half cupful cooked meat of chicken, one-fourth cupful bamboo shoots, a half cupful each of celery and Chinese potatoes, and a little shredded onion.
2. After all is cut, put in bowl and break over it a half dozen eggs.
3. Mix all together and fry in small cakes that are flat like pancakes.
4. Serve on an oval platter and pour over it the following sauce.
5. Mix one teaspoonful of each of the two sauces, a small half teaspoonful of sugar, a teaspoonful of rice flour or cornstarch and a little water in pan in which cakes were fried.
6. Pour sauce over cakes in platter.
7. Serve with rice and tea.
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Foo Yong with Lobster
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cupful cooked lobster meat
- one-fourth cupful bamboo shoots
- a half cupful celery
- a half cupful Chinese potatoes
- a little shredded onion
- a half dozen eggs
- One teaspoonful of each of the two sauces
- a small half teaspoonful of sugar
- a teaspoonful of rice flour or cornstarch
- a little water

## Directions

1. Pull apart one-half cupful of the cooked lobster meat till it is very finely shredded, one-fourth cupful bamboo shoots, a half cupful each of celery and Chinese potatoes, and a little shredded onion.
2. After all is cut, put in bowl and break over it a half dozen eggs.
3. Mix all together and fry in small cakes that are flat like pancakes.
4. Serve on an oval platter and pour over it the following sauce.
5. Mix one teaspoonful of each of the two sauces, a small half teaspoonful of sugar, a teaspoonful of rice flour or cornstarch and a little water in pan in which cakes were fried.
6. Pour sauce over cakes in platter.
7. Serve with rice and tea.
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Foo Yong with Shrimp
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cupful fresh shrimp meat
- one-fourth cupful bamboo shoots
- a half cupful celery
- a half cupful Chinese potatoes
- a little shredded onion
- a half dozen eggs
- One teaspoonful of each of the two sauces
- a small half teaspoonful of sugar
- a teaspoonful of rice flour or cornstarch
- a little water

## Directions

1. Remove the shells from fresh shrimps and use one-half cupful fresh shrimp meat.
2. One-fourth cupful bamboo shoots, a half cupful each of celery and Chinese potatoes, and a little shredded onion.
3. After all is cut, put in bowl and break over it a half dozen eggs.
4. Mix all together and fry in small cakes that are flat like pancakes.
5. Serve on an oval platter and pour over it the following sauce.
6. Mix one teaspoonful of each of the two sauces, a small half teaspoonful of sugar, a teaspoonful of rice flour or cornstarch and a little water in pan in which cakes were fried.
7. Pour sauce over cakes in platter.
8. Serve with rice and tea.
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Food Of The God Of Law Horn
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 pieces bean cake
- 2 cups white nuts
- 2 cups fungus
- A few pieces of bean stick
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 2 cups dry mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut each bean cake into 4 pieces. Fry in boiling oil until nicely brown. Put in cold water and change the water until no oil floats on the top.
2. Soak the fungus, bean stick, and nuts in cold water for 1/6 hour.
3. Cut the bamboo shoots into pieces 1½ inches by 1 inch by 1/16 inch.
4. Put all the ingredients except the mushrooms into an oiled pan and cover with water. Cook for 1 hour.
5. Add the mushrooms and cook for 15 minutes.
6. Add Chinese gravy.

title: Frankfurters
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- small intestine lining of a pig
- lamb
- salt
- sweet sauce
- Fun Wine
- orange skin

## Directions

1. Get the outside lining of the small intestine of a pig. Wash thoroughly with salt and then put into hot water for 10 minutes. Tie one end, force air through from the other end, and tie. Dry in the hot sun.
2. Cut lamb into pieces 3/8 inch by 3/8 inch by 3/8 inch. To every pound of lamb add 3 ounces of salt, 1 tablespoonful of sweet sauce, 1 tablespoonful of Fun Wine, and a few threads of orange skin. Mix well.
3. By means of a funnel fill this mixture into the lining. Put a knob about every six inches. Punch plenty of small holes with a needle.
4. Expose in the hot sun for 1 day. Put in a windy place for 4 days. The air should be dry, so it is best to make these in the fall or winter. Never make them in the summer or spring.
5. Put the frankfurters in a china jar for at least 5 days before using them — the longer, the better.
6. Steam for 3/4 hour before serving. Serve with fried potatoes or fried gray potatoes underneath.

title: Fried Chicken
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 spring chickens, of about 2 pounds each
- Chinese sauce

## Directions

1. Put 3 spring chickens, of about 2 pounds each, into a jar of Chinese sauce for 10 minutes.
2. Place in a pan of boiling oil and fry until the chickens turn yellow.
3. Cut up and serve hot.
4. Use spicery salt on top of the chicken. To make spicery salt, fry together for 15 minutes an equal amount of spicery powder and salt. Have a low fire and turn frequently to prevent burning.

title: Fried Chinese Tomato with Crab
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 crabs
- Chinese tomato equal in weight to crabs, including shell
- 1 tablespoonful Fun Wine
- ⅜ teaspoonful ginger juice
- 1 cup primary soup

## Directions

1. Wash the crabs. Open the shell. Steam until they turn red. By means of a fork take off all the meat. Tear it into small pieces.
2. Fry the tomato in boiling oil until it turns yellow. Then mash into starch.
3. Fry the crab in a hot, oiled pan.
4. Add ginger juice and Fun Wine. Turn often.
5. Add the tomato. Mix well.
6. Add the primary soup. When all is dry, remove from the stove at once.
7. Add salt and red vinegar to improve the taste. Garnish with parsley.

title: Fried Chinese Tomato with Shrimp
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds shrimp
- 6 pounds Chinese tomato
- 1 tablespoonful Fun Wine
- ⅜ teaspoonful ginger juice
- 1 cup primary soup

## Directions

1. Fry the shrimp in a hot, oiled pan.
2. Fry the tomato in boiling oil until it turns yellow. Then mash into starch.
3. Add ginger juice and Fun Wine. Turn often.
4. Add the tomato. Mix well.
5. Add the primary soup. When all is dry, remove from the stove at once.
6. Add salt and red vinegar to improve the taste. Garnish with parsley.

title: Fried Duck Feet
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 pairs of duck feet
- 2 cups chopped bamboo shoots
- 2 cups mushrooms
- 2 cups water chestnuts cut into thin pieces
- salt
- primary soup or water
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Wash thoroughly 20 pairs of duck feet and plunge them into boiling water for 3 minutes. Take off the skin. Take out all bones without spoiling the shape of the feet. Cut each foot into 2 pieces.
2. Cut into small pieces the bamboo shoots, mushrooms, and water chestnuts.
3. Have a hot stove. Oil the frying-pan and fry the feet for 5 minutes. Add salt and the bamboo shoots, chestnuts and mushrooms. Continue to fry for 5 minutes after having mixed well.
4. Add enough primary soup or water to cover all. Cook until tender.
5. Add Chinese gravy and mix well.

title: Fried Eggs Yout Jar Don
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 1 cup dried mushrooms
- 1 1/2 pounds chicken
- A few Chinese onions
- A piece of ginger root

## Directions

1. Boil the eggs until hard. Take off the shell without destroying the shape of the eggs. Now fry them in boiling oil.
2. Cut the mushrooms, chicken, bamboo shoots, and ginger root into threads 1 1/2 inches long. Fry for 5 minutes in a hot, oiled pan.
3. Pour this over the eggs. Add twice more than enough primary soup to cover and cook until 2 cups of liquid remain.
4. Add Chinese gravy.
5. Serve with parsley as a garnish.

title: Fried Fish
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds pike or any fresh water fish
- 1 cup vinegar
- 1 teaspoonful cornstarch

## Directions

1. When the fish has been cleaned, rub inside and out with salt. Then fry in boiling oil until it turns brown.
2. Add enough water to cover. Stir in the vinegar and the cornstarch. Cook for 10 minutes.
3. Add Chinese gravy.

title: Fried Fish Balls
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pounds pike
- 1 1/2 cups salted almonds or peanuts
- 3/4 cup Chinese ham
- 6 pounds vegetables in season
- 1 teaspoonful cornstarch
- 1 cup water
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- 2 cups mushrooms
- 1 egg
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the pike and take out the bones. Run through the grinder three times.
2. Cut into small pieces the almonds, ham, and the vegetables.
3. Put the pike into a big bowl and mix with it the cornstarch, 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoonful salt. Stir well for an hour. Be sure to stir in the same direction all the time.
4. Add the nuts and ham. Mix well.
5. Make the fish balls as described on the preceding page. Add a beaten egg, and boil in boiling oil until they turn yellow.
6. Cook the vegetables and mushrooms until done.
7. Mix together the fish balls, the vegetables, and Chinese gravy.

title: Fried Lamb
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds of lamb cut into pieces 3/4 inch by 1 1/2 inches by 6 inches
- A few drops of Octogon spicery
- 1 bunch garlic
- 1 teaspoonful salt
- Chinese sauce

## Directions

1. Put the lamb, spicery, garlic, and salt into a pan of cold water, more than enough to cover the substances, and cook until tender.
2. Take out the lamb and throw away the rest of the substance. Dip the lamb into a dish of salt and Chinese sauce.
3. Fry the lamb in a pan of boiling oil until brittle.
4. Chop up fried lamb into proper size and serve when hot. Use spicery salt to suit the taste.

title: Fried Noodles
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds Chinese noodles
- 1½ cups onions cut into threads
- 1 cup raw pork cut into threads 1½ inches long
- ¾ cup roast pork cut into threads
- 1 egg fried and cut into threads 1½ inches long
- 2 tablespoonfuls lard
- 1 tablespoonful Chinese sauce
- 1 tablespoonful cornstarch
- 3 cups primary soup
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Put the lard on the noodles, and steam for ¾ hour.
2. After the noodles have been steamed, they tend to stay together; therefore it is necessary to loosen them up with a fork.
3. Place the steamed noodles in a pan of boiling oil and fry until nicely brown. Then put on a dish.
4. Cook the onion and the pork until done.
5. Make a gravy of the primary soup, cornstarch (first dissolved in water), Chinese sauce, and salt and pepper. Add the roast pork and egg threads.
6. Put the onion and pork over the noodles. Pour over this the gravy.

title: Fried Pigeon
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 young pigeons or squabs
- Chinese sauce
- boiling oil
- spicery salt

## Directions

1. Clean 6 young pigeons, or squabs, and put them into a bowl of Chinese sauce.
2. Fry in boiling oil until yellow.
3. Serve when hot with spicery salt.

title: Fried Pigs' Ribs
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds pigs' ribs
- 1 cup vinegar
- 2 teaspoonfuls sugar
- 1 cup Fun Wine
- 2 teaspoonfuls cornstarch
- 1 teaspoonful Chinese sauce
- 1 egg
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Cut the ribs into pieces 3/4 inch by 3/4 inch by 1 3/4 inches, and mix well with the egg, Chinese sauce, salt, 1 teaspoonful cornstarch and 1 teaspoonful sugar. Fry in boiling oil until brown.
2. Add to the mixture the vinegar, Fun Wine, 2 cups of water, and the remaining sugar and cornstarch. Cook until nearly dry.

title: Fried Rice
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 bowls cooked rice
- 3/4 pounds chicken or pork cut into cubes
- 2 cups secondary vegetables
- 5 eggs
- 1 cup primary soup

## Directions

1. Put the rice in a hot, oiled pan and cook until it changes color, turning frequently with a cooking-shovel.
2. Fry the chicken and secondary vegetables for 3 minutes in a hot, oiled pan. Add cold water and cook for 15 minutes. Pour off the water and add the rice.
3. Beat the eggs well and add to the rice.
4. Add the primary soup and continue to cook until the egg appears to be done.

title: Fried Rice (Canton Style)
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- small stalk of celery
- one small onion
- two Chinese potatoes
- one cupful Chinese cured pork
- one good-sized bowl of cooked rice
- well beaten egg
- olive or peanut oil

## Directions

1. Mince fine small stalk of celery, one small onion, two Chinese potatoes, one cupful Chinese cured pork, and mix with one good-sized bowl of cooked rice.
2. Fry in oblong cakes in olive or peanut oil, using sufficient well beaten egg to mold the other ingredients into shape.
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Fried Rice (Chinese Style)
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- small piece smoked pork
- one teaspoonful onion
- one tablespoonful celery
- one medium sized bowl of cooked rice
- peanut oil

## Directions

1. Beat two eggs light and pour over greased pan with a flat bottom, spreading eggs very thin.
2. Take small piece smoked pork, minced fine, one teaspoonful onion minced fine, one tablespoonful celery minced fine.
3. When eggs are cooked, cut in fine pieces.
4. Add all to one medium sized bowl of cooked rice, mix well and fry all together in pan, with peanut oil.
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Fried Shrimp
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds shrimp
- 2 pounds green peppers
- 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoonful cornstarch
- salt
- primary soup
- Chinese gravy
- parsley

## Directions

1. Mix up the shrimp, eggs and cornstarch. Add salt to suit the taste. Put one by one into a pan of boiling oil. Fry until brown.
2. Cut the green peppers into pieces the same size as the shrimp. Fry in an oiled pan for 2 minutes. Then add the shrimp and mix well.
3. Add enough primary soup to cover. Cook for 10 minutes.
4. Add Chinese gravy. Garnish with parsley and serve.

title: Fried Soft-Shell Crab
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 soft-shell crabs
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup red vinegar
- 1 tablespoonful cornstarch

## Directions

1. Mix the crab with the cornstarch and eggs. Salt to taste.
2. Fry in boiling oil until golden brown. Serve with the red vinegar.

title: Fried with Chicken
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup fine cut white meat of cooked chicken
- one small cup inside stalk celery, minced very fine
- one teaspoon finely shredded onion
- two Chinese potatoes peeled and finely cut
- two eggs beaten light
- two tablespoons olive oil
- rice flour
- Chinese sauces

## Directions

1. Spread the beaten eggs over the bottom of a flat pan and cook not too much
2. Cut the cooked eggs in small strips
3. Mix the eggs with the chicken, celery, onion, and potatoes
4. Heat two tablespoons olive oil in a pan until very hot
5. Spread all the ingredients evenly in the pan and fry slowly till light brown
6. Turn with a broad knife and fry on the other side
7. Make sauce by mixing the two sauces and thickening with a little rice flour
8. Serve on platter with sauce
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Gai Mein Gang
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- noodles
- salted water
- good chicken stock
- cooked white meat of chicken
- sliced and cooked Chinese mushrooms
- half cupful of bean sprouts
- fine strips of Chinese cured pork

## Directions

1. Boil noodles till done in salted water and drain in colander
2. Put noodles in bowl and pour over plenty of good chicken stock
3. Have ready cooked white meat of chicken with half as much sliced and cooked Chinese mushrooms
4. Cook bean sprouts for six minutes
5. Put chicken and mushrooms over the noodles in bowl
6. Garnish with fine strips of Chinese cured pork in center, and five thin strips of white meat of chicken each side
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Gold And Silver Egg
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 3 pounds pike
- 1 tablespoonful Chinese ham
- cold boiled water
- salt
- oil
- parsley

## Directions

1. Break the eggs carefully. Put the whites in one bowl and the yolks in another bowl. To each bowl add an equal amount of cold, boiled water and mix well.
2. Grind or hammer the pike (with skin and bones removed). Beat with a little water. Mix well with the whites of the eggs. Salt to suit the taste.
3. Chop the Chinese ham into pieces as small as possible. Mix with the yolks of the eggs. Salt to suit the taste.
4. Now put the Chinese ham on one side of the dish and the pike on the other side. Do not let them mix. Steam for 20 minutes. When done there will be gold color on one side of the dish and silver on the other side.
5. Add oil and parsley before serving.

title: Gold And Silver Frankfurters
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- small intestine lining of a pig
- pork
- pig liver
- salt
- Fun Wine
- orange skin

## Directions

1. Get the outside lining of the small intestine of a pig. Wash thoroughly with salt and then put into hot water for 10 minutes. Tie one end, force air through from the other end, and tie. Dry in the hot sun.
2. Cut pork into pieces 3/8 inch by 3/8 inch by 3/8 inch. To every pound of pork add an equal amount of pig liver, which has been rubbed with salt, washed thoroughly, and cut into pieces the same size as the pork. To this add 3 ounces of salt, 1 tablespoonful of Fun Wine, and a few threads of orange skin. Mix well.
3. By means of a funnel fill this into the lining. Put a knob about every six inches. Punch plenty of small holes with a needle, to let in the air.
4. Expose in the hot sun for 1 day. Put in a windy place for 4 days.
5. Keep them in a china jar for at least 5 days before serving.
6. Steam in a double-boiler for 3/4 hour. Serve with fried potatoes or fried gray potatoes underneath.

title: Gray Potato Pudding
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup dry pork
- 1 cup dry shrimp
- 1 cup dry Chinese olives
- 1 cup dry Chinese frankfurters
- 1 cup dry Chinese onions
- 2 pounds gray potatoes
- 1 pound Chinese gim-flower

## Directions

1. Cut into small pieces the pork, shrimp, olives, frankfurters, and onions.
2. Pare the potatoes. Rub them on a rough grater, or on a board having plenty of nails pointing out 1/16 inch.
3. Mix all of the ingredients together with 2 bowls of plain cold water. Salt to suit the taste.
4. Put into a suitable pan and steam for 1 hour.

title: Green Pepper Beef
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds beef
- 2 cups green peppers cut into pieces 1 inch by 1 inch
- 1 cup celery, chopped
- 1 cup bean sprouts
- 3/4 teaspoonful cornstarch
- 1/2 teaspoonful Fun Wine
- A few pieces sesamum-seed oil
- A few threads ginger root
- Chinese sauce
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the beef into pieces 1/32 inch by 3/4 inch by 1 3/4 inches. Mix with the cornstarch oil, sesamum-seed oil, and Chinese sauce.
2. Put the celery, bean sprouts, and green peppers into a hot, oiled pan and fry for 5 minutes.
3. Add water enough to cover. Spread the beef on top. Cover tightly and cook until nearly dry.
4. Add Chinese gravy and mix well.

title: Ham and Duck
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck
- Chinese ham
- 2 cups primary soup
- 1 cup Fun Wine

## Directions

1. Take the bones out of 1 duck. Then boil the duck for 15 minutes.
2. Boil Chinese ham for 15 minutes and then cut into pieces 1/16 inch by 3/4 inch by 1 1/8 inches. There should be 3/4 as much ham as duck.
3. Put in a bowl 1 piece of duck, 1 piece of ham, and so on, until the bowl is filled.
4. Add 2 cups primary soup and 1 cup Fun Wine, and steam for 2 1/2 hours.

title: Ham and Eggs (Canton Style)
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- lean ham
- three eggs
- Chinese cured pork
- one tablespoonful Seasoning sauce
- one teaspoonful cornstarch
- one tablespoonful hot water

## Directions

1. Cut lean ham into strips about two inches long and one-quarter inch thick.
2. Beat up three eggs very light, add one cupful strips of ham.
3. Fry in small cakes like pancakes and serve on oblong platter.
4. Garnish with strips of Chinese cured pork.
5. Make a sauce of one tablespoonful Seasoning sauce, mixed with one teaspoonful cornstarch and one tablespoonful hot water.
6. Pour into pan and heat till thickened, and put around sides of platter.
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Hard Immortal Food
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 pieces bean cake
- East melon (twice the amount of bean cake)
- 2 cups white nuts

## Directions

1. Cut each bean cake into 4 pieces. Cut the melon into pieces 1 inch by 1 inch by 1½ inches.
2. Fry all in an oiled pan.
3. Add enough water to cover. Add ¼ cake of red cheese and plenty of oil. Mix. Cook for 1 hour.

title: Hot Water Fish
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 pounds cod
- 1 can Chinese Chow Chow
- 1 cup vinegar
- 2 teaspoonfuls salt

## Directions

1. Clean the cod but do not cut it.
2. Pour into a bowl, boiling water equal to 5 times the weight of the fish. Add the salt. Place the fish in this and keep covered until the water gets lukewarm.
3. Put the fish on a suitable plate and pour over it the gravy made of the Chow Chow, vinegar, and a little sugar, cornstarch, and salt. Garnish with parsley and serve.

title: How to Make Tea
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 level teaspoonful of tea
- 1 cup of water

## Directions

1. Heat the water until it just reaches the boiling-point, but no more
2. Insert a thermometer graduated with centigrade degrees; when it reaches 99° remove from the fire at once
3. Pour immediately into a half-pint teapot which contains 1 teaspoonful of Chinese tea
4. Keep covered for three minutes
5. Serve

title: Lamb Chop Suey
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 3/8 pounds lamb
- 25 threads skinless ginger root
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 2 cups dry mushrooms
- 1 cup fried noodles
- Chinese gravy
- primary soup

## Directions

1. Cut all the ingredients except the noodles into threads 1 1/2 inches long.
2. Fry the ginger root, bamboo shoots, and mushrooms in a hot, oiled pan for 10 minutes.
3. Add the lamb. Mix well. Cook for 5 more minutes.
4. Add one bowl of primary soup and cook until 1 cup of liquid is left.
5. Add Chinese gravy.
6. Use the fried noodles for a garnish. Serve when hot.

title: Lamb Hash
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of lamb
- 2 cups water chestnuts
- 2 cups mushrooms
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 1 head lettuce cut into threads
- 2 tablespoonfuls Chinese ham cut into dice
- 1 cup fried noodles
- 1 tablespoonful Fun Wine
