- 3/4 teaspoonful ginger juice
- primary soup

## Directions

1. Fry the chopped lamb in a hot, oiled pan.
2. Add the ginger juice and Fun Wine. Then add the chopped water chestnuts, mushrooms, and bamboo shoots. Mix well.
3. Add enough primary soup to cover and cook until done. Serve on top of the lettuce threads.

title: Lichee Salad
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- ten Mandarin oranges
- ten Lichee nuts
- preserved ginger syrup
- a piece of preserved citron
- water
- two small cucumbers
- lemon juice
- sugar
- rice wafers

## Directions

1. Slice ten Mandarin oranges and remove the skin, seeds, and inner skin
2. Cook the kernels of ten Lichee nuts in preserved ginger syrup for ten minutes
3. Cook a piece of preserved citron in water till tender and mince enough to make two tablespoonfuls
4. Peel and cut two small cucumbers into cubes
5. Mix the cucumbers with the other ingredients
6. Pour over them lemon juice well sweetened with sugar
7. Serve cold with rice wafers
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Lily-Root Pudding
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups lily-root powder
- 1 cup sugar
- 6 cups water

## Directions

1. Dissolve the lily-root powder in a small quantity of cold water. Mash well.
2. To this add the 6 cups of water and the sugar. Stir well.
3. Put into a suitable pan. Steam until done (about 1 hour).

title: Lobster Chop Suey
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lobster (about 6 pounds)
- 9 cups bamboo shoots
- 2 cups water chestnuts
- 3 cups celery
- A few pieces of Chinese onion
- 1 tablespoonful Fun Wine
- 1 teaspoonful ginger root juice
- water

## Directions

1. Boil the lobster in water until it turns red. Take off the shell and cut the meat into fragments not longer than 1½ inches.
2. Fry the lobster with the Fun Wine, and ginger root juice for 5 minutes over a hot fire.
3. Cut the bamboo shoots, water chestnuts, celery, and onion into pieces the size of the lobster fragments. Fry these in a hot, oiled pan for 3 minutes. Add water enough to cover and cook for 10 minutes. Then mix with the lobster.

title: Lobster In Golden Pond
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 eggs
- 3 pounds lobster
- 1 tablespoonful Chinese ham dice
- cold boiled water
- salt
- oil
- parsley
- sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the shell from the lobster. Cut the meat into small pieces.
2. Mix the eggs with an equal amount of cold, boiled water. Beat well. Salt to suit the taste.
3. Put the eggs in a suitable bowl. Spread the lobster on top. Steam for 20 minutes.
4. Garnish with the ham and parsley, and serve with oil and sauce.

title: Lobster Omelet Fo Yung Lung Ha
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 pounds lobster
- 12 eggs
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 2 cups water chestnuts
- 2 cups mushrooms
- 2 onions

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs well in a bowl with oil and salt.
2. Cut the onions, lobster, bamboo shoots, water chestnuts, and mushrooms into threads 1 1/2 inches long. Cook until done.
3. Add beaten eggs and mix well. Continue to cook until eggs are done.

title: Mo Goo War Mein
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- noodles
- salted water
- cooked white meat of chicken
- sliced, canned white mushroom tops
- half cup fine cut Chinese potatoes
- bamboo shoots
- small cup hot chicken stock
- half hard boiled egg
- Chinese cured pork

## Directions

1. Boil noodles till done in salted water and drain in colander
2. Have ready cooked white meat of chicken with sliced, canned white mushroom tops, half cup fine cut Chinese potatoes and bamboo shoots mixed and cooked till tender
3. Put over top of noodles in small bowls, adding small cup hot chicken stock to each bowl
4. Serve with garnish of half hard boiled egg and Chinese cured pork in small thin strips on each side
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Noodle Soup
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds noodles
- 2 teaspoonfuls Chinese sauce
- 1 teaspoonful oil
- A few drops sesamum-seed oil
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Put the noodles into boiling water, and boil until they float on the surface.
2. Quickly place in cold water and stir.
3. Put again into boiling water for 1 minute.
4. Put into a clean bowl containing the oil, sesamum-seed oil, Chinese sauce, and a little pepper.
5. Divide the noodles into individual portions, and into each bowl pour enough boiling primary soup to cover.
6. Garnish, and serve hot.

title: Noodle Soup in Yung Chow Style
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds noodles
- 7 cups primary soup
- 1 cup chicken fragments
- 1½ cups Chinese mushrooms
- 1½ cups Chinese water chestnuts

## Directions

1. Put 2 pounds of noodles into boiling water, and boil until the noodles float on the surface.
2. Take out and place quickly in cold water.
3. Put into boiling oil until hard.
4. Cook in 7 cups primary soup until soft.
5. Make a chicken gravy of 1 cup of chicken fragments, 1½ cups Chinese mushrooms, and 1½ cups Chinese water chestnuts. Add this to Chinese gravy.
6. Pour the gravy into the soup, and stir well. Remove from the stove at once, and serve hot.

title: Noodle Soup with Chicken and Mushrooms
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds noodles
- 2 teaspoonfuls Chinese sauce
- 1 teaspoonful oil
- A few drops sesamum-seed oil
- 1 cup chicken
- 1½ cups mushrooms
- 1½ cups water chestnuts
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Put the noodles into boiling water, and boil until they float on the surface.
2. Quickly place in cold water and stir.
3. Put again into boiling water for 1 minute.
4. Put into a clean bowl containing the oil, sesamum-seed oil, Chinese sauce, and a little pepper.
5. Cut the chicken, mushrooms, and water chestnuts into pieces 1½ inches long. Cook until done. Mix together and add Chinese gravy.
6. Divide the noodles into individual portions, and into each bowl pour enough boiling primary soup to cover.
7. Add the chicken gravy and serve.

title: Noodles
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- one or more eggs
- pinch of salt
- flour
- small spoonful of clear soup stock

## Directions

1. Beat one or more eggs and add a pinch of salt
2. Add as much flour as the egg will absorb
3. Add a small spoonful of clear soup stock and mix with the fingers into a soft, smooth dough, adding more flour as needed
4. Roll out as thin as possible and let stand for a few minutes
5. Roll over and over carefully into a long thin roll
6. Cut thin slices from the end of the roll
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Oyster Chop Suey
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 dozen oysters
- 9 cups bamboo shoots
- 2 cups water chestnuts
- 2 cups celery
- A few pieces of pork
- 1 tablespoonful Fun Wine
- 1 teaspoonful ginger juice
- primary soup
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the oysters, bamboo shoots, chestnuts, celery, and pork into fragments.
2. Put the oysters into boiling water for 10 minutes. Then fry the oysters in a hot oiled pan.
3. Add the Fun Wine and the ginger juice. Continue to cook for 5 minutes.
4. Fry the bamboo shoots, water chestnuts, celery, and pork in an oiled pan for 5 minutes. Add to oysters.
5. Add 1 cup primary soup and boil until about 1 cup of liquid remains.
6. Add Chinese gravy.

title: Pan Chicken
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup lily flower
- 1 cup fungus
- 2 cups dry mushrooms
- 1 chicken
- A small piece of ginger root, without the skin
- 3/4 cup of Fun Wine

## Directions

1. Soak the lily flower, fungus, and the mushrooms in cold water for 3/4 hour.
2. Wash the chicken inside and outside with Chinese sauce. Put in a pan with oil and fry for 10 minutes. Add the Fun Wine and salt. Add 2 cups of water.
3. Now put all into a small pot and add the lily flower, fungus, mushrooms, and ginger. Keep cover on tight and cook for 3/4 hour over a slow fire.

title: Parsley Duck
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ducks
- 2 cups mushrooms
- 2 small pieces ginger root, mashed
- 1 tablespoonful Fun Wine
- Parsley enough to cover the dish 1 inch thick
- primary soup
- cornstarch
- sauce
- sesamum-seed oil
- salt
- Chinese ham

## Directions

1. Open the ducks with one 4-inch cut in the back of each. Take out the interior. Wash thoroughly and dry. Now wash the ducks inside and out with sweet sauce.
2. Cook the duck in a pan of boiling oil until yellow.
3. Put the duck into a frying-pan. Cover with the mushrooms, ginger, and Fun Wine. Add 1/2 more than enough primary soup to cover all. Cook until the duck is done.
4. Make a gravy of the liquid left, with cornstarch, sauce, sesamum-seed oil, and salt.
5. Place the duck on top of the parsley. Pour the gravy over all, and garnish with Chinese ham.

title: Partridge Chop Suey
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 partridges
- 2 cups water chestnuts
- 5 cups mushrooms
- 2 cups celery
- 2 cups primary soup
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Take the bones out of the partridges and cut the meat into thin pieces. Put into a hot, oiled pan and fry for 5 minutes.
2. Cut the secondary vegetables into thin pieces. Put into a hot pan and fry for 2 minutes. Add water and cook for 15 minutes.
3. Add the secondary vegetables to the partridge fragments. Mix well.
4. Add primary soup and cook for 5 minutes.
5. Add Chinese gravy.

title: Partridge Hash
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 partridges
- 2 cups water chestnuts
- 2 cups mushrooms
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 3/8 teaspoonful ginger juice
- 1 teaspoonful Fun Wine
- 1 head lettuce
- 1 tablespoonful ham dice
- 1 cup fried noodles
- primary soup
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the partridges, water chestnuts, mushrooms, and bamboo shoots into very small pieces. Cut the lettuce into threads.
2. Fry the chopped partridges in a hot, oiled pan.
3. Add the ginger juice and Fun Wine.
4. Add the vegetables and mix well.
5. Add enough primary soup to cover and cook until done.
6. Add Chinese gravy and remove from the stove at once. Salt to suit the taste.
7. Serve on top of the lettuce. Use the Chinese ham dice and fried noodles as a garnish.

title: Peanut Candy
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound peanuts
- 3/4 pound sugar

## Directions

1. Fry the nuts in a hot pan for 10 minutes. Take off the skins.
2. Put 1 bowl of water in a hot, oiled pan. To this add the sugar. Cook, stirring constantly, until there is no water left.
3. Mix the peanuts with the sugar on a board. Roll while hot until the mixture is 1/8 inch thick. Let cool.
4. Cut to desired size.

title: Pigeon Chop Suey
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pigeons
- 2 cups water chestnuts
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 2 cups mushrooms
- 2 cups celery
- 2 cups primary soup

## Directions

1. Take the bones out of the pigeons and cut the meat into thin pieces. Put into a hot, oiled pan and fry for 5 minutes.
2. Cut the secondary vegetables into thin pieces. Put into a hot pan and fry for 2 minutes. Add water and cook for 15 minutes.
3. Add the secondary vegetables to the pigeon fragments. Mix well.
4. Add primary soup and cook for 5 minutes.
5. Add Chinese gravy.

title: Pigeon Hash
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pigeons
- 2 cups water chestnuts
- 2 cups mushrooms
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 3/8 teaspoonful ginger juice
- 1 teaspoonful Fun Wine
- 1 head of lettuce
- 1 tablespoonful ham dice
- 1 cup fried noodles
- primary soup
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Cut into very small pieces the pigeons, water chestnuts, mushrooms, and bamboo shoots. Cut the lettuce into threads.
2. Fry the chopped pigeon in a hot, oiled pan.
3. Add the ginger juice and Fun Wine.
4. Add the vegetables and mix well.
5. Add enough primary soup to cover and cook until done.
6. Add Chinese gravy and remove from the stove at once. Salt to suit the taste.
7. Serve on top of the lettuce. Use the Chinese ham dice and fried noodles as a garnish.

title: Pineapple Chicken
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken
- 1 can pineapple
- 2 cups green peppers
- 2 cups celery
- 1 cup canned sour ginger
- 3/8 cup vinegar
- 3/8 cup Fun Wine
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Cut the chicken, pineapple, peppers, celery, and ginger into pieces 1 1/2 inches long.
2. Break the eggs into the chicken and mix well together with a little cornstarch and salt. Then put into boiling oil and fry until all becomes yellow.
3. Put the pineapple, pepper, celery, and ginger into a hot, oiled pan and fry for 2 minutes. Add the fried chicken. Mix well and add the Fun Wine. Cook over a hot fire for 5 minutes; then add enough primary soup to cover and cook until about one cup of liquid is left.
4. Add the vinegar and a little sugar.
5. Pour Chinese gravy over the chicken.

title: Pineapple Duck
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck
- 1 can of pineapple
- 2 cups green peppers
- 2 cups celery
- 4 pieces of canned sour ginger
- 3/4 cup Fun Wine
- 2 eggs
- cornstarch
- salt
- primary soup
- vinegar
- sugar
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Cut into small oblong pieces the duck, pineapple, peppers, celery, and ginger.
2. Break the eggs into the duck and mix well together with a little cornstarch and salt.
3. Put the pineapple, pepper, celery, and ginger into a hot, oiled pan and fry for 2 minutes.
4. Add the fried duck. Mix well and add the Fun Wine. Have a hot fire. After 5 minutes add enough primary soup to cover the substances and cook until about one cup of liquid is left.
5. Add the vinegar and sugar and mix with Chinese gravy.

title: Plain Chop Suey
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds pork
- 2 pounds bean sprouts
- 2 cups onion threads
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the pork into pieces 1/16 inch by 3/8 inch by 1 inch.
2. Put the pork, bean sprouts, and onions into an oiled pan and fry for 10 minutes.
3. Add water enough to cover and cook for 15 minutes.
4. Add Chinese gravy.

title: Plain Omelet Chow Don
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- A little oil
- Salt
- Chinese ham dice
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Break the eggs in a bowl. Add oil and salt. Beat well.
2. Put plenty of oil in a hot frying-pan. Be sure that every part of the pan is covered with oil.
3. Have a medium fire. Place the eggs in the pan and keep turning with a cooking-shovel.
4. When the omelet is done add the Chinese ham dice and parsley for garnish.

title: Pork Hash
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 3/8 pounds pork
- 2 cups water chestnuts
- 2 cups mushrooms
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 1/2 teaspoonful ginger juice
- 1 teaspoonful Fun Wine
- 1 head lettuce cut into threads
- 2 tablespoonfuls Chinese ham cut into dice
- 1 cup fried noodles
- primary soup

## Directions

1. The pork, water chestnuts, mushrooms, and bamboo shoots are to be chopped as fine as possible.
2. Fry the chopped pork in a hot, oiled pan.
3. Add the ginger juice and Fun Wine. Then add the chopped water chestnuts, mushrooms, and bamboo shoots. Mix well.
4. Add enough primary soup to cover and cook until done. Serve on top of the lettuce threads.

title: Pork Omelet Fo Yung York
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 1 pound pork
- 2 onions
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 2 cups water chestnuts
- A little oil
- Salt
- Chinese ham dice
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs well and put them in a bowl with oil and salt.
2. Cut the onions, pork, bamboo shoots, and water chestnuts into threads 1 1/2 inches long.
3. Mix together all of the ingredients and fry in boiling oil until done.
4. Put all in a bowl and add Chinese gravy.

title: Pork Salad
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 3/8 pounds pork (uncut)
- 3 3/8 pounds string beans cut 2 inches long
- 3/8 cup mustard
- 1 cup vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil the pork until done and place in cold water. Punch several small holes with a large needle. Gently squeeze the oil out of it. Change water until no oil is on the top.
2. Cut the pork into thin pieces 1/32 inch by 3/8 inch by 1 3/8 inches. Mix with salt to suit the taste, and with the mustard and vinegar.
3. Boil the string beans for 10 minutes. Put into a bowl, and add salt and enough vinegar.

title: Pork Salad
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 pounds pork (uncut)
- 3/4 pounds string beans cut 2 inches long
- 3/4 cup mustard
- 1 cup vinegar
- salt to taste
- parsley and sage for garnish

## Directions

1. Boil the pork until done and place in cold water. Punch several small holes with a large needle. Gently squeeze the oil out of it. Change water until no oil is on the top.
2. Cut the pork into thin pieces 1/32 inch by 3/4 inch by 1 3/4 inches. Mix with salt to suit the taste, and with the mustard and vinegar.
3. Boil the string beans for 10 minutes. Put into a bowl, and add salt and enough vinegar to cover.
4. Cover and keep air tight for 2 hours.
5. Place the pork on top of the string beans and serve. Use parsley and sage cut into threads for a garnish.

title: Pork Soup
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups lean pork cut into fragments
- 2 teaspoonfuls cornstarch
- 2 teaspoonfuls Chinese sauce
- 5 cups primary soup
- 3 cups Chinese mushrooms
- A few drops sesamum-seed oil
- 3 cups star melon cut into small pieces after the rind is removed
- Salt

## Directions

1. Mix the pork, cornstarch, Chinese sauce, and salt
2. Bring the primary soup to a boil
3. Then add the pork mixture and the mushrooms, and cook for 1/2 hour
4. Ten minutes before taking soup off stove, add the melon

title: Pork Threads
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 3/8 pounds pork
- 3 cups bamboo shoots
- 3 cups mushrooms
- 3 cups primary soup
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Cook the pork until tender and tear off the meat into threads.
2. Cook the bamboo shoots and mushrooms for 20 minutes. Then mix with the pork.
3. Add the primary soup and cook again.
4. Add Chinese gravy.

title: Potato Duck
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck (about 7 pounds)
- The same amount of skinned potatoes
- A cake of Chinese red cheese
- 1 small piece of ginger, pounded
- 3/8 cup Fun Wine
- A piece of green carrot
- water
- oil

## Directions

1. Put the duck into an oiled frying-pan and fry until brown. Turn frequently, so that every part of the duck receives the same amount of heat.
2. Add the ginger and Fun Wine and fry for 2 minutes. Take out and put into a bowl. The liquid left in the frying-pan is to be put into a separate bowl.
3. Add 2 cups of water to the cheese and mash. Rub this well into the duck, inside and outside.
4. Put the potatoes into the cooking-pan. Place the duck on top. Add enough water to cover all. Over this pour any cheese liquid which remains. Add the carrot.
5. Cover the pan and cook until tender.

title: Potato Pork
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds pork
- 4 pounds skinned potatoes
- 1 small piece ginger, pounded
- 1 cake Chinese red cheese
- 3/8 cup Fun Wine
- 1 piece green carrot

## Directions

1. Oil the frying-pan. Put in the pork. Fry until brown. Turn frequently so that every part of the pork receives the same amount of heat.
2. Add the ginger and the wine and fry for 2 minutes.
3. Take out and put into a bowl. Put the juice left in the frying-pan into a separate bowl.
4. Add 2 cups of water to the red cheese and mash.
5. Put the potatoes into a cooking-pan. Add the carrot. Place the pork on top. Add enough water to cover. Pour over this the juice and the cheese.
6. Cover the pan and cook for 1 3/8 hours.

title: Primary Soup
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound chicken
- 1/4 pound lean pork
- 1 pint water
- chicken blood

## Directions

1. Chop the meat into small pieces.
2. Cook slowly for 2½ hours, or until about half of the liquid has evaporated.
3. Put into the mixture a bowl of chicken blood in order to do away with any oil which may exist.
4. Strain through a thick cloth until the liquid is as clear as water. Should there be any oil remaining on top, skim it off.
5. Let the soup cool. Keep in the refrigerator to be used as needed.

title: Quail Hash
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 quails
- 2 cups water chestnuts
- 2 cups mushrooms
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 1/2 teaspoonful ginger juice
- 1 teaspoonful Fun Wine
- 1 head of lettuce
- 1 tablespoonful ham dice
- 1 cup fried noodles
- primary soup
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Cut the quail, water chestnuts, mushrooms, and bamboo shoots into very small pieces. Cut the lettuce into threads.
2. Fry the chopped quails in a hot, oiled pan.
3. Add the secondary vegetables and mix well.
4. Add enough primary soup to cover and cook until done.
5. Add Chinese gravy and remove from the stove at once. Salt to suit the taste.
6. Serve on top of the lettuce. Use the Chinese ham dice and fried noodles as a garnish.

title: Roast Chicken
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 chickens (about 4 pounds each)
- 2 teaspoonfuls spicery powder
- 2 cups Chinese sauce
- A few drops of sesamum-seed oil
- 2 teaspoonfuls salt

## Directions

1. Put the chickens in hot, not boiling, water for 2 minutes and then add the spicery powder, sauce, oil, and salt. Leave the chickens in for 20 minutes. Be sure every bit of chicken is dipped into it.
2. Place the chickens as shown in the diagram, having first started the fire, that the wall is hot. Roast for 1 hour. A hot fire is necessary before the chickens are put into the stove; after the chickens are in, the fire must be kept low. There must be no smoke.

title: Roast Dry Flat Fish
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 pounds dry flat fish
- 1 tablespoonful peanut oil
- 1 cup red vinegar
- sesamum-seed oil
- sugar

## Directions

1. Wash the dry flat fish thoroughly. Rub over it a coating of oil or lard. Place on top of a charcoal fire by means of a wire. It is done when there are bubbles on the surface. Turn over and continue the same. Be careful not to let it get burnt.
2. Tear the fish into threads. Mix with these threads the sesamum-seed oil, peanut oil, and red vinegar. Sugar to suit the taste.

title: Roast Duck
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ducks (about 4 pounds each)
- 2 teaspoonfuls spicery powder
- 2 cups Chinese sauce
- A few drops of sesamum-seed oil
- 2 teaspoonfuls salt

## Directions

1. Put the ducks in hot, not boiling, water for 2 minutes and then add the spicery powder, sauce, oil, and salt. Leave the ducks in for 20 minutes. Be sure every bit of duck is dipped into it.
2. Place the ducks as shown in the diagram, having first started the fire, that the wall is hot. Roast for 1 hour. A hot fire is necessary before the ducks are put into the stove; after the ducks are in, the fire must be kept low. There must be no smoke.

title: Roast Duck Biscuits
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck
- 2 pounds flour
- 3/4 cup lard
- 1 tablespoonful salt
- 4 1/2 tablespoonfuls baking powder
- cold water

## Directions

1. Roast the duck, take out the bones, and cut the meat into small pieces.
2. Sift the flour and put into a suitable bowl. Mix the flour with enough cold water to make a thin dough.
3. Roll the dough out flat and cut into the size of biscuits.
4. Now roll the biscuits flat and fill them with the duck meat, wrapping the dough around the meat into a perfect ball. Close it up carefully. The amount of duck should be one-half the amount of dough.
5. Place in a double-boiler for 10 minutes before putting over the fire. Steam for 3/4 of an hour.

title: Roast Goose
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 goose
- 1 teaspoonful spicery powder
- 1 cup Chinese sauce
- A few drops sesamum-seed oil
- 1 teaspoonful salt

## Directions

1. Put the goose in hot, not boiling, water for 2 minutes and then add the spicery powder, sauce, oil, and the salt. Leave the goose in for 20 minutes. Be sure every bit of goose is dipped into it.
2. Place the goose as shown in cut on page 50. Start the fire before this, so that the wall is hot. Let the goose roast for 1 hour. A hot fire is necessary before the goose is put in. Afterward have a low fire. There must be no smoke.

title: Roasted Oysters
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ dozen oysters
- 1 cup red vinegar
- Sesamum-seed oil
- Sugar
- Salt

## Directions

1. Dry the oysters with a clean, dry cloth. Then dip in oil.
2. String the oysters through a wire and roast until done over a charcoal fire. Have them about 1 foot above the fire. Serve with a sauce made of the red vinegar, sesamum-seed oil, sugar, and salt.

title: Ruby Mixed With Pearls
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 bean cakes
- 1 pound Chinese ham
- 1 pound shrimp
- primary soup
- Chinese gravy
- parsley

## Directions

1. Remove the thin outer layer from the bean cakes.
2. Cut the ham and the shrimp into ¼ inch cubes.
3. Boil all together in a pan of primary soup until the shrimp turns red.
4. Add Chinese gravy.
5. Garnish with parsley.

title: Salt Chicken
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 whole chicken dried well inside and out
- Rock salt enough to cover the chicken
- 3/4 cup of spicery salt

## Directions

1. Put the salt into a hot pan and leave over the fire until the salt gets very hot.
2. In a suitable pan bury the chicken in this hot salt, covering the chicken entirely. Cover the pan and keep air-tight for 2 hours.
3. Take out the chicken and shake off all salt.
4. Cut up the chicken and sprinkle the spicery salt over it. Chicken prepared in this way tastes delicious, and is not salty.

title: Sesamum-Seed Candy
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 4 ounces cornstarch
- 2 handfuls sesamum-seeds

## Directions

1. Oil pan well. Pour into it 1 bowl of water and then the sugar and cornstarch. Cook until no water is left.
2. Roll out on a board sprinkled with the sesamum-seeds. Roll into balls or bars. Let cool.

title: Shark Fins
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of dry shark fins
- a few pieces of dry garlic
- 2 pieces of ginger root
- 2 tablespoonfuls of lard
- primary soup
- 1 cup of chicken starch
- whites of 3 eggs
- Chinese ham dice
- cornstarch
- salt
- 1 tablespoonful red vinegar
- parsley

## Directions

1. Buy 2 pounds of dry shark fins from a Chinese grocery store. Soak in cold water for 3 hours.
2. Boil the fins with a few pieces of dry garlic and 2 pieces of ginger root. Change the water several times when boiling.
3. Put into a suitable pan. Add 2 tablespoonfuls of lard, and twice more than enough primary soup to cover. Boil slowly for 3/4 hour. Drain off the liquid and throw it away.
4. Put into another pan, containing 6 pints of primary soup, and boil.
5. Change again into a third pan of primary soup. Add gravy which consists of 1 cup of chicken starch, the whites of 3 eggs, Chinese ham dice and a little cornstarch and salt. Use 1 tablespoonful red vinegar to improve the taste. Garnish with parsley and serve hot.

title: Shrimp Chop Suey
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 pounds shrimp with the shells off
- 2 cups dry mushrooms
- 2 cups bean sprouts
- 1 piece of onion cut into threads
- 3/4 teaspoonful Fun Wine
- primary soup
- Chinese gravy

## Directions

1. Fry mushrooms, bean sprouts, and onion in an oily pan for 10 minutes. Add enough water to cover. Boil for 15 minutes.
2. Fry the shrimp in a hot, oiled pan and add the Fun Wine. Keep frying until the shrimp turns red.
3. Mix the shrimp with the mushrooms, bean sprouts, and onion, and add 2 cups of primary soup. When it boils, add Chinese gravy.

title: Shrimp In Golden Pond
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 7 eggs
- 1 pound shrimp
- 1 tablespoonful Chinese ham dice
- cold boiled water
- salt
- oil
- parsley
- sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the shrimp. Cut the meat into small pieces.
2. Mix the eggs with an equal amount of cold, boiled water. Beat well. Salt to suit the taste.
3. Put the eggs in a suitable bowl. Spread the shrimp on top. Steam for 20 minutes.
4. Garnish with the ham and parsley, and serve with oil and sauce.

title: Shrimp Omelet Fo Yung Har
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 eggs
- 1 pound shrimp
- 2 onions
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 2 cups water chestnuts
- 2 cups mushrooms

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs well and put them in a bowl with oil and salt.
2. Cut the onions, shrimp, bamboo shoots, water chestnuts, and mushrooms into threads 1 1/2 inches long.
3. Mix together all of the ingredients and fry in boiling oil until done.
4. Put all in a bowl and add Chinese gravy.

title: Soft Immortal Food
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 pieces bean cake
- 2 cups dry mushrooms
- 4 ounces Chinese vermicelli
- A few bean sticks

## Directions

1. Cut each piece of bean cake into 4 pieces.
2. Soak the mushrooms, vermicelli, and bean sticks in water for 1½ hour. Cut the vermicelli into pieces 3 inches long.
3. Put all of the ingredients in cold water and cook for 1 hour. Put in plenty of oil and red cheese.
4. Add Chinese gravy.

title: Spiced Pork
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- pork
- spicery salt
- salt
- sugar
- sauce residue
- sweet sauce
- Fun Wine
- Chinese tissue-paper

## Directions

1. To each pound of pork use 2 ounces of spicery salt, 1 1/2 teaspoonfuls of salt, 1 1/2 teaspoonfuls of sugar. Mix and rub over the pork. Expose to the hot sun for 1 day. Dip into 5 ounces of sauce residue. Mash. Add 1 1/2 ounces of sweet sauce, 2 ounces of good Fun Wine. Mix well.
2. Wrap up with Chinese tissue-paper, and put in a dry place where it can stay until dry. It is now ready for cooking.

title: Spiced Pork With Gray Potatoes
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- spiced pork
- gray potatoes
- Chinese onions

## Directions

1. Cut the pork and the potatoes into pieces 1 inch by 1 inch by 1 1/2 inches.
2. Cook the pork, the potatoes and the onions in plain water in an oiled pan for 3/4 hour.
3. Add Chinese gravy.

title: Steamed Chestnut Pork
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 3/8 pounds pork cut into pieces 1 inch by 3/8 inch by 1 3/8 inches
- 2 cups skinless chestnuts
- 1 cup Fun Wine
- sweet sauce

## Directions

1. Dip the pork into sweet sauce and fry in boiling oil until red.
2. Take out the pork and place in cold water. Change water until no oil is on top of the water.
3. Dry fried pork and place in a bowl.
4. Add the chestnuts and the Fun Wine. Steam for 1 3/8 hours.

title: Steamed Chicken
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken cut into pieces about 1 1/2 inches long
- 2 cups mushrooms
- A few slices of ginger root
- 4 pieces of red dates
- 1 Chinese onion
- Cornstarch, sauce, salt, and oil

## Directions

1. Mix well the chicken, oil, salt, cornstarch, and sauce.
2. Add mushrooms, ginger root, and red dates. Put on a plate and steam for 1 1/2 hour.
3. Add Chinese onion cut into threads 1 1/2 inches long. Garnish with parsley. Serve with mustard, sauce, and oil.

title: Steamed Duck
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck (about 8 pounds)
- 2 cups chestnuts without skins
- 1 cup barley
- 1/2 cup white nuts without skins
- 1 tablespoonful Fun Wine
- cold water
- Chinese sauce
- salt
- oil

## Directions

1. Soak the chestnuts and barley in cold water for 1 hour.
2. Open the back of the duck with one cut about 4 inches long. Take out the bones without tearing the meat.
3. When the duck has been cleaned wash it inside and out with Chinese sauce. Salt thoroughly and put into a hot, oiled pan and fry for 10 minutes.
4. Add the Fun Wine. Turn the duck frequently.
5. Now put the duck, with the open side upward, in a bowl a little larger than the duck. Fill with the chestnuts, barley, and white nuts and any juice left in the frying-pan.
6. Steam for 2½ hours.
7. Pour off the gravy into another bowl. Skim off the oil.
8. Transfer the duck carefully into a larger bowl by holding the bowls mouth to mouth.
9. Pour the gravy over the duck and serve.

title: Steamed Lamb
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of lamb cut into pieces 3/8 inch by 1 3/8 inches by 3/8 inch
- 2 cups dry mushrooms
- 2 cups unskinned chestnuts
- 3 red dates
- 1 cup Fun Wine
- salt and sweet sauce (Chinese name Chew Tout)
- primary soup

## Directions

1. Use water to boil the lamb for 10 minutes.
2. Take out the lamb and dip into a bowl of salt and sweet sauce (Chinese name Chew Tout).
3. Put all into a suitable bowl. Add enough primary soup to cover and add the chestnuts, red dates, and Fun Wine. Steam until tender.
4. Remove any oil from the liquid, and serve.

title: Steamed Oysters
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ dozen oysters
- Lard-skin
- 1 teaspoonful ginger juice
- 1 tablespoonful Fun Wine
- Whites of 2 eggs
- lettuce

## Directions

1. Wash the oysters. Boil them in water for 10 minutes. Then fry in hot oil.
2. Add the Fun Wine and ginger juice. Fry for 7 minutes.
3. Cut the lard-skin into pieces as wide as the oysters and about 4 inches long. Wrap each oyster with a piece of lard-skin, using the white of egg for paste.
4. Put the wrapped oysters into boiling oil and fry until they are brown.
5. Now set them in a bowl and steam for 1½ hours. Serve on lettuce.

title: Steamed Pike
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 pounds pike
- 2 pieces Chinese onion
- 1 cup Chinese mushrooms
- 100 threads ginger root
- The same amount of pork

## Directions

1. Clean the fish but do not cut it. Rub with salt. Add 1 teaspoonful oil.
2. Cut the onion, mushrooms, ginger root, and pork into threads, 1½ inches long. Spread this on top of the fish and steam for 1¼ hour. Garnish with parsley.

title: Steamed Shrimp
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 large shrimps
- 48 pieces bamboo shoots
- 1 tablespoonful Fun Wine
- 1 teaspoonful ginger root juice
- primary soup
- gravy

## Directions

1. Remove the shells from the shrimps and cut into halves. Fry in a hot oiled pan.
2. Mix together the Fun Wine and the ginger root juice. Add this liquid to the shrimps. Cook for 5 minutes. Drain off the liquid but do not throw it away.
3. The 48 pieces of bamboo shoots should be the same size as the halves of the shrimp, 1/16 inch long. Place the shrimp and bamboo shoots in a bowl, arranging them — first a piece of shrimp and then a piece of bamboo shoot, and so on — having them parallel.
4. Add 3/4 cup primary soup and steam for 2 hours.
5. Turn now into another bowl by placing the two bowls mouth to mouth. Add gravy and serve.

title: Steamed Turtle
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 turtles
- 2 cups mushrooms
- 2 cups chestnuts
- ¾ pound Chinese roast pork
- ½ cup Fun Wine
- 1 teaspoonful ginger root juice

## Directions

1. Put the live turtles into a cooking-pan of cold water. Bring to a boil slowly.
2. Remove the shell and interior. Wash. Cut the meat into pieces 1 inch by 1 inch by 1½ inches.
3. Have a hot fire. Put 2 tablespoonfuls of oil into a frying-pan. When the oil is very hot put in the meat, Fun Wine, and ginger root juice. Fry for 10 minutes. Turn constantly.
4. Cut the bamboo shoots into pieces ¾ inch by 1½ inches by 1/16 inch. Cut the pork into pieces ¾ inch by 1¼ inches by ¾ inch.
5. Add to the meat in the frying-pan, the mushrooms, chestnuts, bamboo shoots, pork and enough primary soup to cover. Bring to a boil.
6. Put all into a suitable bowl and steam for 1½ hours.
7. Skim off any oil which may be on the top. Salt to taste.

title: Stuffed Egg
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- shrimp or crab meat chopped fine
- Chinese ham, diced
- chicken, chopped fine
- water chestnuts or Chinese onions chopped fine
- oil
- sesamum-seed oil
- sauce

## Directions

1. Oil enough egg cups. Break the eggs carefully into these, putting in the whites first. Now put the unbeaten yolk in the center of the cup, letting it float on top. Steam the eggs until done.
2. With a spoon carefully take off the yolk of the egg.
3. Fill the egg with the chicken and other ingredients, well mixed. Steam about 20 minutes.
4. Serve with sesamum-seed oil and sauce.

title: Stuffed Green Peppers
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 green peppers
- 3 pounds pike, ground
- 2 tablespoonfuls Chinese ham dice
- 2 Chinese onions, diced
- ¾ cup salted almonds, diced
- salt water
- boiling oil
- primary soup
- Chinese sauce
- sesamum-seed oil
- sweet sauce
- cornstarch
- parsley

## Directions

1. Put the pike in a small amount of salt water and stir for ¾ hour. Then mix with the ham, onions, and almonds.
2. Cut the peppers into halves, take out the seeds, and fill with the pike, ham, etc. Fry in boiling oil until yellow.
3. Cook in primary soup for ¾ hour.
4. Add a gravy made of Chinese sauce, a few drops of sesamum-seed oil, sweet sauce, and cornstarch.
5. Garnish with parsley.

title: Stuffed Squash
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 squash
- 3 pounds pike, ground
- 2 tablespoonfuls Chinese ham dice
- 2 Chinese onions, diced
- ¾ cup salted almonds, diced
- salt water
- boiling oil
- primary soup
- Chinese sauce
- sesamum-seed oil
- sweet sauce
- cornstarch
- parsley

## Directions

1. Put the pike in a small amount of salt water and stir for ¾ hour. Then mix with the ham, onions, and almonds.
2. Remove the seeds from the squash and fill with the pike, ham, etc. Fry in boiling oil until yellow.
3. Cook in primary soup for ¾ hour.
4. Add a gravy made of Chinese sauce, a few drops of sesamum-seed oil, sweet sauce, and cornstarch.
5. Garnish with parsley.

title: Stuffed Triangle Bean Cake
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 bean cakes
- 3 pounds pike
- ¾ cup Chinese ham dice
- ¾ cup Chinese onion dice
- ¾ cup salted almonds
- boiling oil
- primary soup
- Chinese sauce
- sesamum-seed oil
- sweet sauce
- cornstarch
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut each bean cake into two triangles. Cut the triangles and remove the pieces to leave a hole as shown in the instructions.
2. Grind or pound the pike. Stir up in a small amount of salt water for ¾ hour.
3. Mix with the ham, onions, and almonds.
4. Fill the hole in the bean cake with this mixture. Fry in boiling oil until yellow.
5. Cook the fried bean cake triangles in primary soup for ¾ hour.
6. Add a gravy made of Chinese sauce, a few drops of sesamum-seed oil, sweet sauce, and cornstarch.
7. Garnish with parsley.

title: Turtle Soup
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 turtles
- 2 cups mushrooms
- 2 cups chestnuts
- ¾ pound Chinese roast pork
- ¾ cup Fun Wine
- 1 teaspoonful ginger root juice
- 25 cents' worth of Bug Kay

## Directions

1. Put the live turtles into a cooking-pan of cold water. Bring to a boil slowly.
2. Remove the shell and interior. Wash. Cut the meat into pieces 1 inch by 1 inch by 1¼ inches.
3. Have a hot fire. Put 2 tablespoonfuls of oil into a frying-pan. When the oil is very hot put in the meat, Fun Wine, and ginger root juice. Fry for 10 minutes. Turn constantly.
4. Cut the bamboo shoots into pieces ¾ inch by 1½ inches by 1/16 inch. Cut the pork into pieces ¾ inch by 1¼ inches by ¾ inch.
5. Add to the meat in the frying-pan, the mushrooms, chestnuts, bamboo shoots, pork and enough primary soup to cover. Bring to a boil.
6. Put into a suitable bowl, add the Bug Kay, Dong Sum, Gay Gee, Yen York and steam for 1½ hours.
7. Skim off any oil which may be on the top. Salt to taste. Serve the soup in bowls.

title: Vegetable Soup
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- Any kind of vegetables washed thoroughly and cut into pieces one inch long
- Small piece of root ginger
- 9 cups primary soup
- Salt
- Oil

## Directions

1. Heat the cooking pan for 1/2 minute
2. Then spread one teaspoonful of oil all over the surface of the pan and let it heat for from 1 to 2 minutes
3. Add the salt, then the vegetables and ginger
4. Turn over frequently until the volume of the vegetables is reduced to 1/2
5. Add the primary soup, and let it cook until it boils
6. Keep the soup boiling slowly until done; the length of time depends upon the kind of vegetables
7. It is best to keep the pan uncovered

title: Walnut Chicken
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 cups English walnuts
- 2 cups onions cut into dice
- 2 cups water chestnuts cut into dice
- 1 1/2 cups celery cut into dice
- 1 pound of chicken without bones

## Directions

1. Remove the skins from the walnuts by placing them in boiling water. Then fry them in boiling oil until they turn yellow.
2. Put the secondary vegetables in a hot cooking-pan containing oil and fry for 2 or 3 minutes. Keep them moving so that each particle receives the same amount of heat. Add enough primary soup to cover and boil for 15 minutes.
3. Put the chicken in a hot, oiled pan and fry for 2 minutes.
4. Add the vegetables.
5. Add Chinese gravy and remove from the stove.
6. Place on a dish and garnish with the fried walnuts.

title: War Mein
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of any kind of meat
- two handfuls Chinese mushrooms
- two Spanish onions
- Seasoning Sauce
- one pound peeled and sliced Chinese potatoes
- two cupfuls noodles
- one egg
- cooked ham
- green onion tops

## Directions

1. Cut meat into small square pieces
2. Soak Chinese mushrooms for two hours, remove the stems and cut in fine pieces
3. Cut Spanish onions fine
4. Put all in pan with enough Seasoning Sauce to cover bottom of pan about one inch deep
5. When boiling, cover tightly and set on back of stove to cook slowly about two hours
6. When nearly done, put in one pound peeled and sliced Chinese potatoes and let cook till done, about ten minutes
7. Add a little boiling water if needed, but not too much
8. Cook noodles in boiling water and drain in colander
9. Put meat mixture in large oval dish and cover over top with two cupfuls cooked noodles
10. Beat one egg very light, cook in flat pan spreading very thin, and let cool
11. Slice egg in thin, fine strips
12. Slice green onion tops and a piece of cooked ham about three inches long
13. Put egg strips over center of noodles with ham each side and green onion at ends of dish
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Water Chestnut Pudding
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 cups water chestnut powder
- 1 cup sugar
- 6 cups water

## Directions

1. Dissolve the water chestnut powder in a little cold water. Mash well.
2. Now add the sugar and the 6 cups of water. Stir well.
3. Put into a suitable pan. Steam until done (about 1 hour).

title: Why Shon Partridge
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 partridges
- A few pieces of pork
- 10 cents' worth of Why Shon
- 10 cents' worth of Kee Zee
- 25 cents' worth of Dong Chong Chow
- primary soup
- oil
- Chinese sauce

## Directions

1. When the partridges have been washed, rub inside and out with salt. Put in a bowl.
2. Wash the Why Shon, Kee Zee, Dong Chong Chow, and pork. Add to the partridges.
3. Cover with primary soup and steam for 3 1/2 hours in a double-boiler.
4. Remove the oil from the top of the liquid. Salt to taste. Use the liquid for soup.
5. Serve the partridge with oil and Chinese sauce.

title: Why Shon Pigeon
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 pigeons
- A few pieces of pork
- 10 cents' worth of Kee Zee
- 25 cents' worth of Dong Chong Chow
- 10 cents' worth of Why Shon

## Directions

1. When the pigeons have been washed, rub inside and out with salt. Put in a bowl.
2. Wash the Why Shon, Kee Zee, Dong Chong Chow, and pork. Add to the pigeons.
3. Cover with primary soup and steam for 1½ hours in a double-boiler.
4. Remove the oil from the top of the liquid. Salt to taste. Use the liquid for soup. Serve the pigeon with oil and Chinese sauce.

title: Wild Duck
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck
- 2 cups chestnuts without skins
- 2 cups water chestnuts
- 1 cup chopped pork
- 1 cup barley
- 3/4 cup white nuts without skins
- Chinese sauce
- salt
- Fun Wine

## Directions

1. Soak the chestnuts and the barley in cold water for 1 hour.
2. Open the back of the duck with one cut about 4 inches long. Take out the bones without tearing the meat.
3. Wash the duck inside and out with Chinese sauce. Salt thoroughly and put into an oiled pan and fry for 10 minutes. Add the Fun Wine. Turn the duck frequently. Take from the stove and put into a bowl a little larger than the duck.
4. Fill the duck with the chestnuts, barley, nuts and any juice left in the frying-pan. Steam for 2 1/4 hours.
5. Get a bowl larger than the one holding the duck and place that on top of the other one, mouth to mouth. Use a cloth to protect the hand from getting burned. Carefully pour the gravy off into a third bowl and take off any oil. Turn the duck upside down so it will fall into the new bowl.
6. Pour the gravy over the duck and serve.

title: Wine Vapor Duck
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ducks (about 4 pounds each)
- 3/8 cups, 3/8 full, of any wine
- 1 teaspoonful spicery salt
- 2 tablespoonfuls Chinese ham

## Directions

1. Open the ducks with one cut about 4 inches long in the back of each.
2. When the ducks have been cleaned, place them in a suitable bowl with the back upward.
3. Rub the duck inside and out with spicery salt.
4. Place carefully in each duck one of the cups of wine. Be sure the wine does not spill on the duck.
5. Steam in a double-boiler for 2½ hours, or until tender.
6. Remove cover. Pour in cold water to stop the steam. By means of a cloth take out the cups of wine.
7. Pour off the gravy into another bowl. Skim off the oil.
8. Place over the bowl containing the duck a second bowl a little larger than the first. Turn the first bowl upside down so that the duck falls into the second bowl.
9. Now pour the gravy back over the duck. Garnish with the Chinese ham cut into small oblong pieces and with a little parsley.

title: Winkle
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 winkles
- 2 cups water chestnuts, unshelled
- 8 cups mushrooms
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 1 cup celery
- A few pieces of pork
- 3/8 teaspoonful ginger root juice
- 1 tablespoonful Fun Wine
- 1 cup primary soup
- water

## Directions

1. Wash the winkles carefully. Break the shell. Take the meat out. Cut into pieces 1/16 inch by 1 1/4 inches by 1 5/16 inches.
2. Cut the secondary vegetables and the pork into pieces and fry in a hot, oiled pan. Add enough water to cover. Cover the pan and cook until done.

title: Winkle Chow Hung Lor
author: Shiu Wong Chan
body:
## Ingredients

- Winkles
- 2 cups water chestnuts, unshelled
- 3 cups mushrooms
- 2 cups bamboo shoots
- 1 cup celery
- A few pieces of pork
- 3/8 teaspoonful ginger root juice
- 1 tablespoonful Fun Wine
- 1 cup primary soup

## Directions

1. Wash the winkles carefully. Break the shell. Take the meat out. Cut into pieces 1/16 inch by 1 1/4 inches by 1 3/16 inches.
2. Cut the secondary vegetables and the pork into pieces and fry in a hot, oiled pan. Add enough water to cover. Cover the pan and cook until done.
3. Fry the meat over a quick fire in a hot, oiled pan. Add the ginger root juice and the Fun Wine.
4. Now add the secondary vegetables and mix well.
5. Add the primary soup and Chinese gravy.
6. Serve with Chinese ham dice and parsley for garnish.

title: Yar Mein
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- three large cupfuls of noodles
- salted water or chicken stock with a little onion cut from the green tops
- good chicken stock
- one-half as much freshly cooked Chop Sooy as there are noodles
- shredded green onion

## Directions

1. Cook noodles till done in salted water or chicken stock with a little onion cut from the green tops
2. Drain in fine colander and place in five small bowls or one large one
3. Pour over a little good chicken stock already heated
4. Add one-half as much freshly cooked Chop Sooy as there are noodles over top
5. Garnish with shredded green onion
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen

title: Yet Gai Mein
author: Jessie Louise Nolton
body:
## Ingredients

- noodles
- salted water
- chicken stock or beef or mutton broth
- one-half a hard boiled egg for each bowl
- five or six fine strips of Chinese cured pork
- small pieces of cooked white meat of chicken
- Seasoning Sauce

## Directions

1. Boil the noodles till done in salted water and drain in colander
2. Put noodles in the necessary number of bowls to serve with chicken stock, or beef, or mutton broth, enough to thin somewhat
3. Use for garnish one-half a hard boiled egg for each bowl with five or six fine strips of the Chinese cured pork and a few small pieces of the cooked white meat of chicken around the sides of bowl
4. Additional seasoning may be supplied at the table by using a small portion of the Seasoning Sauce, according to the taste of the individual
source_title: Chinese Cookery in the Home Kitchen
